    49	  ##
    50	  # Requires an optional runtime dependency
    51	  # @raise [LLM::DependencyError]
    52	  #  When the dependency cannot be loaded
    53	  def self.require(name)
    54	    super
    55	  rescue ::LoadError
    56	    names = {"xchan" => "xchan.rb", "net/http/persistent" => "net-http-persistent"}
    57	    name = names[name] || name
    58	    raise LLM::LoadError,
    59	      "#{name} is an optional runtime dependency but it does not appear to be installed. " \
    60	      "Consider 'gem install #{name}', adding '#{name}' to your Gemfile or " \
    61	      "opting out of the functionality provided by '#{name}'"
    62	  end

    64	  ##
    65	  # @param [Symbol, LLM::Provider] llm
    66	  #  The name of a provider, or an instance of LLM::Provider
    67	  # @return [LLM::Object]
    68	  def self.registry_for(llm)
    69	    lock(:registry) do
    70	      name = Symbol === llm ? llm : llm.name
    71	      @registry[name] ||= Registry.for(name)
    72	    end
    73	  end

    85	  ##
    86	  # Sets the JSON adapter used by the library
    87	  # @note
    88	  #  This should be set once from the main thread when your program starts.
    89	  #  Defaults to {LLM::JSONAdapter::JSON LLM::JSONAdapter::JSON}.
    90	  # @param [Class, String, Symbol] adapter
    91	  #  A JSON adapter class or its name
    92	  # @return [void]
    93	  def json=(adapter)
    94	    @json = case adapter.to_s
    95	    when "JSON", "json" then JSONAdapter::JSON
    96	    when "Oj", "oj" then JSONAdapter::Oj
    97	    when "Yajl", "yajl" then JSONAdapter::Yajl
    98	    else
    99	      is_class = Class === adapter
   100	      is_subclass = is_class && adapter.ancestors.include?(LLM::JSONAdapter)
   101	      if is_subclass
   102	        adapter
   103	      else
   104	        raise TypeError, "Adapter must be a subclass of LLM::JSONAdapter"
   105	      end
   106	    end
   107	  end

     3	##
     4	# The Provider class represents an abstract class for
     5	# LLM (Language Model) providers.
     6	#
     7	# @abstract
     8	class LLM::Provider
     9	  include LLM::Transport::Execution
    10	
    11	  ##
    12	  # @param [String, nil] key
    13	  #  The secret key for authentication
    14	  # @param [String] host
    15	  #  The host address of the LLM provider
    16	  # @param [Integer] port
    17	  #  The port number
    18	  # @param [Integer] timeout
    19	  #  The number of seconds to wait for a response
    20	  # @param [Boolean] ssl
    21	  #  Whether to use SSL for the connection
    22	  # @param [String] base_path
    23	  #  Optional base path prefix for HTTP API routes.
    24	  # @param [Boolean] persistent
    25	  #  Whether to use a persistent connection.
    26	  #  Requires the net-http-persistent gem.
    27	  # @param [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] transport
    28	  #  Optional override with any {LLM::Transport} instance or subclass.
    29	  def initialize(key:, host:, port: 443, timeout: 60, ssl: true, base_path: "", persistent: false, transport: nil)
    30	    @key = key
    31	    @host = host
    32	    @port = port
    33	    @timeout = timeout
    34	    @ssl = ssl
    35	    @base_path = LLM::Utils.normalize_base_path(base_path)
    36	    @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
    37	    @headers = {"User-Agent" => "llm.rb v#{LLM::VERSION}"}
    38	    @transport = LLM::Transport::Utils.resolve_transport(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:, transport:, persistent:)
    39	    @monitor = Monitor.new
    40	  end

    47	    "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @key=[REDACTED] @transport=#{transport.inspect} @tracer=#{tracer.inspect}>"
    48	  end

    98	  ##

   181	
   182	    # Interact with the context via the chat completions API.
   183	    # This method immediately sends a request to the LLM and returns the response.
   184	    #
   185	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
   186	    # @param params The params, including optional :role (defaults to :user), :stream, :tools, :schema etc.
   187	    # @return [LLM::Response] Returns the LLM's response for this turn.
   188	    # @example
   189	    #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
   190	    #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
   191	    #   res = ctx.talk("Hello, what is your name?")
   192	    #   puts res.messages[0].content
   193	    def talk(prompt, params = {})
   194	      @owner = @llm.request_owner
   195	      compactor.compact!(prompt) if compactor.compact?(prompt)
   196	      repair!(@messages, prompt)
   197	      prompt, params, res = mode == :responses ? respond(prompt, params) : complete(prompt, params)
   198	      self.compacted = false
   199	      role = params[:role] || @llm.user_role
   200	      role = @llm.tool_role if params[:role].nil? && [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
   201	      @messages.concat LLM::Prompt === prompt ? prompt.to_a : [LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
   202	      @messages.concat [res.choices[-1]]
   203	      res
   204	    end

   206	    ##
   207	    # Ask a question and return the content string directly.
   208	    # Accepts `with:` for file attachments and a block for streaming.
   209	    # This interface is compatible with RubyLLM's `ask` method.
   210	    # @param [String] prompt
   211	    # @param [Hash] options
   212	    # @option options [String, Array<String>, nil] :with
   213	    #  File path(s) to attach
   214	    # @option options [#<<, LLM::Stream, nil] :stream
   215	    #  A stream target
   216	    # @yield [String] content chunks when streaming
   217	    # @return [LLM::Response]
   218	    def ask(prompt, options = {}, &block)
   219	      options = {with: nil, stream: nil}.merge!(options || {})
   220	      with, stream = options.values_at(:with, :stream)
   221	      prompt = with ? [prompt, [*with].map { local_file(_1) }] : prompt
   222	      target = if block
   223	        blk = block.dup
   224	        blk.singleton_class.alias_method(:<<, :call)
   225	        blk
   226	      else
   227	        stream
   228	      end
   229	      target ? talk(prompt, stream: target) : talk(prompt)
   230	    end
   231	
   232	    ##
   233	    # @return [String]
   234	    def inspect
   235	      "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} " \
   236	      "@llm=#{@llm.class}, @mode=#{@mode.inspect}, @params=#{@params.inspect}, " \
   237	      "@messages=#{@messages.inspect}>"
   238	    end
   239	
   240	    ##
   241	    # Returns an array of functions that can be called
   242	    # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
   243	    def functions
   244	      return_ids = returns.map(&:id)
   245	      @messages
   246	        .select(&:assistant?)
   247	        .flat_map do |msg|
   248	          fns = msg.functions.select { _1.pending? && !return_ids.include?(_1.id) }
   249	          fns.each do |fn|
   250	            fn.tracer = tracer
   251	            fn.model  = msg.model
   252	          end
   253	        end.extend(LLM::Function::Array)
   254	    end
   255	
   256	    ##
   257	    # Returns whether there is pending tool work in this context.
   258	    # This prefers queued streamed tool work when present, and otherwise
   259	    # falls back to unresolved functions derived from the message history.
   260	    # @return [Boolean]
   261	    def functions?
   262	      pending = queue
   263	      (pending && !pending.empty?) || functions.any?
   264	    end
   265	
   266	    ##
   267	    # Spawns a function through the context.
   268	    #
   269	    # When a guard is configured, this method can return an in-band guarded
   270	    # tool error instead of spawning work.
   271	    #
   272	    # @param [LLM::Function] function
   273	    # @param [Symbol] strategy
   274	    # @return [LLM::Function::Return, LLM::Function::Task]
   275	    def spawn(function, strategy)
   276	      warning = guard&.call(self)
   277	      return guarded_return_for(function, warning) if warning
   278	      function.spawn(strategy)
   279	    end
   280	
   281	    ##
   282	    # Returns tool returns accumulated in this context
   283	    # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
   284	    def returns
   285	      @messages
   286	        .select(&:tool_return?)
   287	        .flat_map do |msg|
   288	          LLM::Function::Return === msg.content ?
   289	            [msg.content] :
   290	            [*msg.content].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
   291	        end
   292	    end
   293	
   294	    ##
   295	    # Waits for queued tool work to finish.
   296	    #
   297	    # This prefers queued streamed tool work when the configured stream
   298	    # exposes a non-empty queue. Otherwise it falls back to waiting on
   299	    # the context's pending functions directly.
   300	    #
   301	    # @param [Symbol, Array<Symbol>] strategy
   302	    #  If the stream queue already has tool work, `wait` will drain it
   303	    #  without using this argument.
   304	    #  Otherwise, this controls how pending functions are resolved directly.
   305	    #  Use `:call` for sequential execution without spawning.
   306	    # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] except
   307	    #  A list of functions to exclude from the wait
   308	    # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
   309	    def wait(strategy, except: [])
   310	      if LLM::Stream === stream && !stream.queue.empty?
   311	        @queue = stream.queue
   312	        @queue.wait
   313	      else
   314	        tools  = except.empty? ? functions : functions - except
   315	        guards = guarded_returns(tools:)
   316	        return guards if guards
   317	        @queue = tools.spawn(strategy)
   318	        returns = @queue.wait
   319	        emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
   320	        returns
   321	      end
   322	    ensure
   323	      @queue = nil
   324	      @stream = nil
   325	    end
   326	
   327	    ##
   328	    # Interrupt the active request, if any.
   329	    # This is inspired by Go's context cancellation model.
   330	    # @return [nil]
   331	    def interrupt!
   332	      pending = functions.to_a
   333	      llm.interrupt!(@owner)
   334	      queue&.interrupt!
   335	      return if pending.empty?
   336	      pending.each(&:interrupt!)
   337	      returns = pending.map { _1.cancel(reason: "function call cancelled") }
   338	      @messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, returns)
   339	      nil
   340	    end
   341	    alias_method :cancel!, :interrupt!
   342	
   343	    ##
   344	    # Returns token usage accumulated in this context
   345	    # @return [LLM::Object]
   346	    def usage
   347	      if usage = @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.usage
   348	        LLM::Object.from(
   349	          input_tokens: usage.input_tokens || 0,
   350	          output_tokens: usage.output_tokens || 0,
   351	          reasoning_tokens: usage.reasoning_tokens || 0,
   352	          input_audio_tokens: usage.input_audio_tokens || 0,
   353	          output_audio_tokens: usage.output_audio_tokens || 0,
   354	          input_image_tokens: usage.input_image_tokens || 0,
   355	          cache_read_tokens: usage.cache_read_tokens || 0,
   356	          cache_write_tokens: usage.cache_write_tokens || 0,
   357	          total_tokens: usage.total_tokens || 0
   358	        )
   359	      else
   360	        ZERO_USAGE
   361	      end
   362	    end
   363	
   364	    ##
   365	    # Build a role-aware prompt for a single request.
   366	    #
   367	    # Prefer this method over {#build_prompt}. The older
   368	    # method name is kept for backward compatibility.
   369	    # @example
   370	    #   prompt = ctx.prompt do
   371	    #     system "Your task is to assist the user"
   372	    #     user "Hello, can you assist me?"
   373	    #   end
   374	    #   ctx.talk(prompt)
   375	    # @param [Proc] b
   376	    #  A block that composes messages. If it takes one argument,
   377	    #  it receives the prompt object. Otherwise it runs in prompt context.
   378	    # @return [LLM::Prompt]
   379	    def prompt(&b)
   380	      LLM::Prompt.new(@llm, &b)
   381	    end
   382	    alias_method :build_prompt, :prompt
   383	
   384	    ##
   385	    # Recongize an object as a URL to an image
   386	    # @param [String] url
   387	    #  The URL
   388	    # @return [LLM::Object]
   389	    #  Returns a tagged object
   390	    def image_url(url)
   391	      LLM::Object.from(value: url, kind: :image_url)
   392	    end
   393	
   394	    ##
   395	    # Recongize an object as a local file
   396	    # @param [String] path
   397	    #  The path
   398	    # @return [LLM::Object]
   399	    #  Returns a tagged object
   400	    def local_file(path)
   401	      LLM::Object.from(value: LLM.File(path), kind: :local_file)
   402	    end
   403	
   404	    ##
   405	    # Reconginize an object as a remote file
   406	    # @param [LLM::Response] res
   407	    #  The response
   408	    # @return [LLM::Object]
   409	    #  Returns a tagged object
   410	    def remote_file(res)
   411	      LLM::Object.from(value: res, kind: :remote_file)
   412	    end
   413	
   414	    ##
   415	    # @return [LLM::Tracer]
   416	    #  Returns an LLM tracer
   417	    def tracer
   418	      @llm.tracer
   419	    end
   420	
   421	    ##
   422	    # @return [LLM::Stream, #<<, nil]
   423	    #  Returns a stream object, or nil
   424	    def stream
   425	      @stream || @params[:stream]
   426	    end
   427	
   428	    ##
   429	    # Returns the model a Context is actively using
   430	    # @return [String]
   431	    def model
   432	      messages.find(&:assistant?)&.model || @params[:model]
   433	    end
   434	
   435	    ##
   436	    # @return [Hash]
   437	    def to_h
   438	      {
   439	        schema_version: 1,
   440	        model:,
   441	        compacted:,
   442	        messages: @messages.map { serialize_message(_1) }
   443	      }
   444	    end
   445	
   446	    ##
   447	    # @return [String]
   448	    def to_json(...)
   449	      LLM.json.dump(to_h, ...)
   450	    end
   451	
   452	    ##
   453	    # Save the current context state
   454	    # @example
   455	    #  llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
   456	    #  ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
   457	    #  ctx.talk "Hello"
   458	    #  ctx.save(path: "context.json")
   459	    # @raise [SystemCallError]
   460	    #  Might raise a number of SystemCallError subclasses
   461	    # @return [void]
   462	    def serialize(path:)
   463	      ::File.binwrite path, LLM.json.dump(to_h)
   464	    end
   465	    alias_method :save, :serialize
   466	
   467	    ##
   468	    # @return [LLM::Cost]
   469	    #  Returns an _approximate_ cost for a given context
   470	    #  based on both the provider, and model
   471	    def cost
   472	      LLM::Cost.from(self)
   473	    end
   474	
   475	    ##
   476	    # Returns the model's context window.
   477	    # The context window is the maximum amount of input and output
   478	    # tokens a model can consider in a single request.
   479	    # @note
   480	    #   This method returns 0 when the provider or
   481	    #   model can't be found within {LLM::Registry}.
   482	    # @return [Integer]
   483	    def context_window
   484	      LLM
   485	        .registry_for(llm)
   486	        .limit(model:)
   487	        .context
   488	    rescue LLM::NoSuchModelError, LLM::NoSuchRegistryError
   489	      0
   490	    end
   491	
   492	    private
   493	
   494	    ##
   495	    # Binds runtime metadata onto an active stream.
   496	    # @api private
   497	    def bind!(stream, model, tools)
   498	      return unless LLM::Stream === stream
   499	      @stream = stream
   500	      stream.extra[:ctx] = self
   501	      stream.extra[:tracer] = tracer
   502	      stream.extra[:model] = model
   503	      stream.extra[:tools] = tools
   504	    end
   505	
   506	    ##
   507	    # Returns the bound stream queue, if available.
   508	    # @api private
   509	    def queue
   510	      [@queue, stream&.queue].compact.first
   511	    rescue NoMethodError
   512	      nil
   513	    end
   514	
   515	    ##
   516	    # Loads skill directories and adapts them into tools.
   517	    # @api private
   518	    def load_skills(skills)
   519	      [*skills].map { LLM::Skill.load(_1).to_tool(self) }
   520	    end
   521	
   522	    ##
   523	    # Builds in-band guarded returns when the guard blocks tool work.
   524	    # @api private
   525	    def guarded_returns(tools:)
   526	      warning = guard&.call(self)
   527	      return unless warning
   528	      tools.map { guarded_return_for(_1, warning) }
   529	    end
   530	
   531	    ##
   532	    # Rewrites a prompt and params through the configured transformer.
   533	    # @api private
   534	    def transform(prompt, params)
   535	      return [prompt, params] unless transformer
   536	      stream = params[:stream]
   537	      stream.on_transform(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
   538	      transformer.call(self, prompt, params)
   539	    ensure
   540	      stream.on_transform_finish(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
   541	    end
   542	
   543	    ##
   544	    # Executes a turn through the Responses API.
   545	    # @api private
   546	    def respond(prompt, params)
   547	      params = @params.merge(params)
   548	      prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
   549	      bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
   550	      res_id = params[:store] == false ? nil : @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.response&.response_id
   551	      params = params.merge(previous_response_id: res_id, input: @messages.to_a).compact
   552	      [prompt, params, @llm.responses.create(prompt, params)]
   553	    end
   554	
   555	    ##
   556	    # Executes a turn through the chat completions API.
   557	    # @api private
   558	    def complete(prompt, params)
   559	      params = params.merge(messages: @messages.to_a)
   560	      params = @params.merge(params)
   561	      prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
   562	      bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
   563	      [prompt, params, @llm.complete(prompt, params)]
   564	    end
   565	
   566	    ##
   567	    # Builds one guarded tool return for a blocked function call.
   568	    # @api private
   569	    def guarded_return_for(function, warning)
   570	      LLM::Function::Return.new(function.id, function.name, {
   571	        error: true,
   572	        type: LLM::GuardError.name,
   573	        message: warning
   574	      })
   575	    end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The {LLM::Transport LLM::Transport} class defines the execution
     6	  # interface used by {LLM::Provider}.
     7	  #
     8	  # Custom transports can subclass this class and override {#request} to
     9	  # execute provider requests without changing request adapters or
    10	  # response adapters.
    11	  #
    12	  # Providers should construct {LLM::Transport::Request} objects before
    13	  # delegating to a transport. Custom transports can execute those
    14	  # requests directly, or transform them into backend-specific request
    15	  # objects before execution.
    16	  #
    17	  # Only {#request} is required. The remaining methods are optional hooks
    18	  # for features such as interruption, request ownership, or persistence,
    19	  # and only need to be implemented when the underlying adapter can
    20	  # support them.
    21	  #
    22	  # Returned responses should implement the
    23	  # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} interface. In
    24	  # practice this can mean adapting another client's response object so
    25	  # existing provider execution, response adapters, and error handlers
    26	  # can rely on one normalized response contract instead of
    27	  # transport-specific classes.
    28	  class Transport
    29	    require_relative "transport/request"
    30	    require_relative "transport/response"
    31	    require_relative "transport/utils"
    32	    require_relative "transport/stream_decoder"
    33	    require_relative "transport/net_http_adapter"
    34	    require_relative "transport/http"
    35	    require_relative "transport/persistent_http"
    36	    require_relative "transport/curb"
    37	    require_relative "transport/execution"
    38	
    39	    ##
    40	    # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP transport class.
    41	    # @return [Class]
    42	    def self.net_http
    43	      HTTP
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    ##
    47	    # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP::Persistent transport class.
    48	    # @return [Class]
    49	    def self.net_http_persistent
    50	      PersistentHTTP
    51	    end
    52	
    53	    ##
    54	    # Returns the optional libcurl (curb) transport class.
    55	    # Requires the `curb` gem.
    56	    # @return [Class]
    57	    def self.curb
    58	      Curb
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    ##
    62	    # Performs a request through the transport.
    63	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
    64	    # @param [Object] owner
    65	    # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream
    66	    # @yieldparam [LLM::Transport::Response] response
    67	    # @return [Object]
    68	    def request(request, owner:, stream: nil, &)
    69	      raise NotImplementedError
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # Returns the current request owner.
    74	    # @return [Object]
    75	    def request_owner
    76	      return Fiber.current unless defined?(::Async)
    77	      Async::Task.current? ? Async::Task.current : Fiber.current
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    ##
    81	    # Returns the exception classes that indicate an interrupted request.
    82	    # @return [Array<Class<Exception>>]
    83	    def interrupt_errors
    84	      []
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    ##
    88	    # Interrupt an active request, if any.
    89	    # @param [Object] owner
    90	    # @return [nil]
    91	    def interrupt!(owner)
    92	      raise NotImplementedError
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    ##
    96	    # Returns whether an execution owner was interrupted.
    97	    # @param [Object] owner
    98	    # @return [Boolean, nil]
    99	    def interrupted?(owner)
   100	      nil
   101	    end
   102	
   103	    ##
   104	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
   105	    # @param [IO] io
   106	    # @return [void]
   107	    def set_body_stream(request, io)
   108	      request.body_stream = io
   109	      request["transfer-encoding"] = "chunked" unless request["content-length"]
   110	    end
   111	  end
   112	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::Transport
     4	  ##
     5	  # {LLM::Transport::Request LLM::Transport::Request} defines the
     6	  # normalized request interface expected by transports.
     7	  #
     8	  # Providers build request objects through this class, then hand them
     9	  # to a transport for execution without depending on any specific HTTP
    10	  # client library.
    11	  class Request
    12	    ##
    13	    # @return [Object, nil]
    14	    attr_accessor :body
    15	
    16	    ##
    17	    # @return [IO, nil]
    18	    attr_accessor :body_stream
    19	
    20	    ##
    21	    # @return [String]
    22	    attr_reader :method
    23	
    24	    ##
    25	    # @return [String]
    26	    attr_reader :path
    27	
    28	    ##
    29	    # @return [Hash]
    30	    attr_reader :headers
    31	
    32	    ##
    33	    # @param [String] path
    34	    # @param [Hash, nil] headers
    35	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
    36	    def self.get(path, headers = nil)
    37	      new("GET", path, headers)
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    ##
    41	    # @param [String] path
    42	    # @param [Hash, nil] headers
    43	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
    44	    def self.post(path, headers = nil)
    45	      new("POST", path, headers)
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    ##
    49	    # @param [String] path
    50	    # @param [Hash, nil] headers
    51	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
    52	    def self.put(path, headers = nil)
    53	      new("PUT", path, headers)
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    ##
    57	    # @param [String] path
    58	    # @param [Hash, nil] headers
    59	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
    60	    def self.patch(path, headers = nil)
    61	      new("PATCH", path, headers)
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    ##
    65	    # @param [String] path
    66	    # @param [Hash, nil] headers
    67	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
    68	    def self.delete(path, headers = nil)
    69	      new("DELETE", path, headers)
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # @param [String] method
    74	    # @param [String] path
    75	    # @param [Hash, nil] headers
    76	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
    77	    def initialize(method, path, headers = nil)
    78	      @method = method.to_s.upcase
    79	      @path = path.to_s
    80	      @headers = {}
    81	      (headers || {}).each { self[_1] = _2 }
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    ##
    85	    # @param [String] key
    86	    # @return [String, nil]
    87	    def [](key)
    88	      @headers[normalize_header(key)]
    89	    end
    90	
    91	    ##
    92	    # @param [String] key
    93	    # @param [Object] value
    94	    # @return [String]
    95	    def []=(key, value)
    96	      @headers[normalize_header(key)] = value.to_s
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    ##
   100	    # @yieldparam [String] key
   101	    # @yieldparam [String] value
   102	    # @return [Hash]
   103	    def each_header(&block)
   104	      @headers.each(&block)
   105	    end
   106	
   107	    ##
   108	    # @return [String]
   109	    def inspect
   110	      "#<#{self.class.name}:0x#{object_id.to_s(16)}" \
   111	        " @method=#{@method} @path=#{@path}" \
   112	        " @headers=#{@headers.inspect}>"
   113	    end
   114	
   115	    private
   116	
   117	    def normalize_header(key)
   118	      key.to_s.downcase
   119	    end
   120	  end
   121	end

     3	class LLM::Transport
     4	  ##
     5	  # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} defines the
     6	  # normalized HTTP response interface expected by transports and
     7	  # provider error handlers.
     8	  #
     9	  # Custom transports can execute requests through any underlying HTTP
    10	  # client, then adapt that client's native response object to this
    11	  # interface.
    12	  #
    13	  # This keeps the transport boundary focused on one contract:
    14	  # providers, execution, and error handlers only need a response
    15	  # object that implements
    16	  # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response}, regardless of
    17	  # how the request was actually performed.

    97	    def not_found?
    98	      raise NotImplementedError
    99	    end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The OpenAI class implements a provider for
     6	  # [OpenAI](https://platform.openai.com/).
     7	  #
     8	  # @example
     9	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    10	  #   require "llm"
    11	  #
    12	  #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
    13	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    14	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    15	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    16	  class OpenAI < Provider
    17	    require_relative "openai/error_handler"
    18	    require_relative "openai/request_adapter"
    19	    require_relative "openai/response_adapter"
    20	    require_relative "openai/stream_parser"
    21	    require_relative "openai/models"
    22	    require_relative "openai/responses"
    23	    require_relative "openai/images"
    24	    require_relative "openai/audio"
    25	    require_relative "openai/files"
    26	    require_relative "openai/moderations"
    27	    require_relative "openai/vector_stores"
    28	
    29	    include RequestAdapter
    30	
    31	    HOST = "api.openai.com"
    32	
    33	    ##
    34	    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    35	    def initialize(base_path: "/v1", **)
    36	      super(host: HOST, base_path:, **)
    37	    end
    38	
    39	    ##
    40	    # @return [Symbol]
    41	    #  Returns the provider's name
    42	    def name
    43	      :openai
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    ##
    47	    # Provides an embedding
    48	    # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create OpenAI docs
    49	    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    50	    # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    51	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    52	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    53	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    54	    def embed(input, model: "text-embedding-3-small", **params)
    55	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path("/embeddings"), headers)
    56	      req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:, model:}.merge!(params))
    57	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
    58	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
    59	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
    60	      res
    61	    end
    62	
    63	    ##
    64	    # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
    65	    # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI docs
    66	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    67	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    68	    # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    69	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    70	    # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
    71	    #  When given an object a provider does not understand
    72	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    73	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    74	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    75	      req, messages = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
    76	      tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
    77	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
    78	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    79	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    80	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
    81	      res
    82	    end

    84	    ##

    89	      LLM::OpenAI::Responses.new(self)
    90	    end

   141	    ##
   142	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
   143	    def assistant_role
   144	      "assistant"
   145	    end

   148	    # Returns the default model for chat completions
   149	    # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/gpt-4.1 gpt-4.1
   150	    # @return [String]
   151	    def default_model
   152	      "gpt-4.1"
   153	    end
   154	
   155	    ##
   156	    # @note
   157	    #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
   158	    #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
   159	    #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
   160	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
   161	    def server_tools
   162	      {
   163	        web_search: server_tool(:web_search),
   164	        file_search: server_tool(:file_search),
   165	        image_generation: server_tool(:image_generation),
   166	        code_interpreter: server_tool(:code_interpreter),
   167	        computer_use: server_tool(:computer_use)
   168	      }
   169	    end
   170	
   171	    ##
   172	    # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
   173	    # OpenAI web search tool.
   174	    # @example
   175	    #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
   176	    #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
   177	    #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
   178	    # @param query [String] The search query.
   179	    # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
   180	    def web_search(query:)
   181	      ResponseAdapter.adapt(
   182	        responses.create(query, store: false, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]),
   183	        type: :web_search
   184	      )
   185	    end
   186	
   187	    private
   188	
   189	    def completions_path
   190	      path("/chat/completions")
   191	    end
   192	
   193	    def headers
   194	      lock do
   195	        (@headers || {}).merge(
   196	          "Content-Type" => "application/json",
   197	          "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
   198	        )
   199	      end
   200	    end
   201	
   202	    def stream_parser
   203	      LLM::OpenAI::StreamParser
   204	    end
   205	
   206	    def error_handler
   207	      LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler
   208	    end
   209	
   210	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   211	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
   212	      tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   213	      params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   214	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   215	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   216	      if params[:stream]
   217	        params[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}.merge!(params[:stream_options] || {})
   218	      end
   219	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   220	    end
   221	
   222	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
   223	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   224	      body = LLM.json.dump({messages: adapt(messages, mode: :complete).flatten}.merge!(params))
   225	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)
   226	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   227	      [req, messages]
   228	    end
   229	
   230	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   231	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   232	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
   233	      else
   234	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
   235	      end
   236	    end
   237	
   238	    def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
   239	      message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
   240	      value = message&.content || fallback
   241	      value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
   242	    end
   243	  end
   244	end

     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Anthropic class implements a provider for
     6	  # [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com).
     7	  #
     8	  # @example
     9	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    10	  #   require "llm"
    11	  #
    12	  #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
    13	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    14	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    15	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    16	  class Anthropic < Provider
    17	    require_relative "anthropic/utils"
    18	    require_relative "anthropic/error_handler"
    19	    require_relative "anthropic/request_adapter"
    20	    require_relative "anthropic/response_adapter"
    21	    require_relative "anthropic/stream_parser"
    22	    require_relative "anthropic/models"
    23	    require_relative "anthropic/files"
    24	    include RequestAdapter
    25	    extend Utils
    26	
    27	    HOST = "api.anthropic.com"
    28	
    29	    ##
    30	    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    31	    def initialize(**)
    32	      super(host: HOST, **)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @return [Symbol]
    37	    #  Returns the provider's name
    38	    def name
    39	      :anthropic
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    ##
    43	    # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
    44	    # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages Anthropic docs
    45	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    46	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    47	    # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    48	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    49	    # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
    50	    #  When given an object a provider does not understand
    51	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    52	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    53	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    54	      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
    55	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
    56	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    57	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    58	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
    59	      res
    60	    end
    61	
    62	    ##
    63	    # Provides an interface to Anthropic's models API
    64	    # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/models-list
    65	    # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Models]
    66	    def models
    67	      LLM::Anthropic::Models.new(self)
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    ##
    71	    # Provides an interface to Anthropic's files API
    72	    # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/files Anthropic docs
    73	    # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Files]
    74	    def files
    75	      LLM::Anthropic::Files.new(self)
    76	    end
    77	
    78	    ##
    79	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
    80	    def assistant_role
    81	      "assistant"
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    ##
    85	    # Anthropic expects tool results to be sent as user messages
    86	    # containing `tool_result` content blocks rather than a distinct
    87	    # `tool` role.
    88	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
    89	    def tool_role
    90	      :user
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    ##
    94	    # Returns the default model for chat completions
    95	    # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/all-models#model-comparison-table claude-sonnet-4-20250514
    96	    # @return [String]
    97	    def default_model
    98	      "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    99	    end
   100	
   101	    ##
   102	    # @note
   103	    #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
   104	    #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
   105	    #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
   106	    # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/web-search-tool Anthropic docs
   107	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
   108	    def server_tools
   109	      {
   110	        bash: server_tool(:bash, type: "bash_20250124"),
   111	        web_search: server_tool(:web_search, type: "web_search_20250305", max_uses: 5),
   112	        text_editor: server_tool(:str_replace_based_edit_tool, type: "text_editor_20250728", max_characters: 10_000)
   113	      }
   114	    end
   115	
   116	    ##
   117	    # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
   118	    # Anthropic web search tool.
   119	    # @example
   120	    #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
   121	    #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
   122	    #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
   123	    # @param query [String] The search query.
   124	    # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
   125	    def web_search(query:)
   126	      ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]), type: :web_search)
   127	    end
   128	
   129	    private
   130	
   131	    def headers
   132	      lock do
   133	        (@headers || {}).merge(
   134	          "Content-Type" => "application/json",
   135	          "x-api-key" => @key,
   136	          "anthropic-version" => "2023-06-01",
   137	          "anthropic-beta" => "files-api-2025-04-14"
   138	        )
   139	      end
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    def stream_parser
   143	      LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser
   144	    end
   145	
   146	    def error_handler
   147	      LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler
   148	    end
   149	
   150	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   151	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 1024}.merge!(params)
   152	      tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   153	      params = [params, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   154	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   155	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   156	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   157	    end
   158	
   159	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
   160	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   161	      payload = adapt(messages)
   162	      body = LLM.json.dump(payload.merge!(params))
   163	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/messages", headers)
   164	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   165	      req
   166	    end
   167	
   168	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   169	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   170	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
   171	      else
   172	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
   173	      end
   174	    end
   175	  end
   176	end

     4	  ##
     5	  # The Google class implements a provider for
     6	  # [Gemini](https://ai.google.dev/). The Google provider
     7	  # can accept multiple inputs (text, images, audio, and video).
     8	  # The inputs can be provided inline via the prompt for files
     9	  # under 20MB or via the Gemini Files API for files
    10	  # that are over 20MB.
    11	  #
    12	  # @example
    13	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    14	  #   require "llm"
    15	  #
    16	  #   llm = LLM.google(key: ENV["KEY"])
    17	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    18	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    19	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    20	  class Google < Provider
    21	    require_relative "google/utils"
    22	    require_relative "google/error_handler"
    23	    require_relative "google/request_adapter"
    24	    require_relative "google/response_adapter"
    25	    require_relative "google/stream_parser"
    26	    require_relative "google/models"
    27	    require_relative "google/images"
    28	    require_relative "google/audio"
    29	    require_relative "google/files"

    32	    extend Utils

    49	    ##
    50	    # Provides an embedding
    51	    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    52	    # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    53	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    54	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    55	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    56	    def embed(input, model: "gemini-embedding-001", **params)
    57	      model = model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
    58	      path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", "embedContent?key=#{@key}"].join(":")
    59	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
    60	      req.body = LLM.json.dump({content: {parts: [{text: input}]}})
    61	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
    62	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
    63	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
    64	      res
    65	    end

    77	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    78	      params, stream, tools, role, model = normalize_complete_params(params)
    79	      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
    80	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model:)
    81	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    82	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    83	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model:, res:, span:)
    84	      res
    85	    end

    98	    # @return [see LLM::Google::Images]
    99	    def images

   151	    ##

   183	          "Content-Type" => "application/json"
   184	        )

   188	    def stream_parser

   192	    def error_handler

   195	
   196	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   197	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
   198	      tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   199	      params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   200	      role, model, stream = [:role, :model, :stream].map { params.delete(_1) }
   201	      [params, stream, tools, role, model]
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
   205	      action = stream ? "streamGenerateContent?key=#{@key}&alt=sse" : "generateContent?key=#{@key}"
   206	      model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
   207	      path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", action].join(":")
   208	      req  = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
   209	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   210	      body = LLM.json.dump({contents: adapt(messages)}.merge!(params))
   211	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   212	      req
   213	    end
   214	
   215	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   216	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   217	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
   218	      else
   219	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
   220	      end
   221	    end
   222	  end
   223	end

     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
     6	  # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
     7	  # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
     8	  # that can process both images and text.
     9	  #
    10	  # @example
    11	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    12	  #   require "llm"
    13	  #
    14	  #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
    15	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
    16	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    17	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    18	  class Ollama < Provider
    19	    require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
    20	    require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
    21	    require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
    22	    require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
    23	    require_relative "ollama/models"
    24	
    25	    include RequestAdapter
    26	
    27	    HOST = "localhost"
    28	
    29	    ##
    30	    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    31	    def initialize(**)
    32	      super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @return [Symbol]
    37	    #  Returns the provider's name
    38	    def name
    39	      :ollama
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    ##
    43	    # Provides an embedding
    44	    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    45	    # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    46	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    47	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    48	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    49	    def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
    50	      params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
    51	      req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
    52	      req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
    53	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
    54	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
    55	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
    56	      res
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    ##
    60	    # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
    61	    # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
    62	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    63	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    64	    # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    65	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    66	    # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
    67	    #  When given an object a provider does not understand
    68	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    69	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    70	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    71	      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
    72	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
    73	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    74	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    75	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
    76	      res
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    ##
    80	    # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
    81	    # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
    82	    # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
    83	    def models
    84	      LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    ##
    88	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
    89	    def assistant_role
    90	      "assistant"
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    ##
    94	    # Returns the default model for chat completions
    95	    # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
    96	    # @return [String]
    97	    def default_model
    98	      "qwen3:latest"
    99	    end
   100	
   101	    private
   102	
   103	    def headers
   104	      lock do
   105	        (@headers || {}).merge(
   106	          "Content-Type" => "application/json",
   107	          "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
   108	        )
   109	      end
   110	    end
   111	
   112	    def stream_parser
   113	      LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
   114	    end
   115	
   116	    def error_handler
   117	      LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   121	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
   122	      tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   123	      params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   124	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   125	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   126	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   127	    end
   128	
   129	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
   130	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   131	      body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
   132	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
   133	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   134	      req
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   138	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   139	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
   140	      else
   141	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	  end
   145	end

     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
     6	  # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
     7	  #
     8	  # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
     9	  # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
    10	  # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
    11	  # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
    12	  #
    13	  # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
    14	  # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
    15	  # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
    16	  # instead of a single API key.
    17	  #
    18	  # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
    19	  # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
    20	  # Ruby's stdlib.
    21	  #
    22	  # @example
    23	  #   require "llm"
    24	  #
    25	  #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
    26	  #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
    27	  #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
    28	  #     region: "us-east-1"
    29	  #   )
    30	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    31	  #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
    32	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
    33	  class Bedrock < Provider
    34	    require_relative "bedrock/signature"
    35	    require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
    36	    require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
    37	    require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"
    38	    require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter"
    39	    require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/completion"
    40	    require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/models"
    41	    require_relative "bedrock/stream_decoder"
    42	    require_relative "bedrock/stream_parser"
    43	    require_relative "bedrock/models"
    44	
    45	    include RequestAdapter
    46	
    47	    HOST_PATTERN = "bedrock-runtime.%s.amazonaws.com"
    48	
    49	    ##
    50	    # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
    51	    # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
    52	    # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
    53	    # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
    54	    # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
    55	    # @param [Integer] port Connection port
    56	    # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
    57	    # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
    58	    def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
    59	                   region: nil, session_token: nil,
    60	                   host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
    61	                   **)
    62	      region ||= "us-east-1"
    63	      @access_key_id = access_key_id
    64	      @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
    65	      @aws_region = region
    66	      @session_token = session_token
    67	      host ||= HOST_PATTERN % region
    68	      @aws_host = host
    69	      super(key: @access_key_id, host:, port:, ssl:, timeout:, persistent: false)
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # @return [Symbol] Returns the provider's name
    74	    def name
    75	      :bedrock
    76	    end
    77	
    78	    ##
    79	    # Provides an interface to the Bedrock Converse API
    80	    #
    81	    # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_Converse.html
    82	    #
    83	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    84	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    85	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    86	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    87	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    88	      req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
    89	      tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
    90	      sign!(req, body)
    91	      model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
    92	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
    93	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    94	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    95	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
    96	      res
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    ##
   100	    # Provides an interface to Bedrock's ListFoundationModels API.
   101	    #
   102	    # @note
   103	    #  Unlike the Converse API (bedrock-runtime), this endpoint lives
   104	    #  on the control plane (bedrock.<region>.amazonaws.com).
   105	    #
   106	    # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_ListFoundationModels.html
   107	    # @return [LLM::Bedrock::Models]
   108	    def models
   109	      LLM::Bedrock::Models.new(self)
   110	    end
   111	
   112	    ##
   113	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   114	    def files
   115	      raise NotImplementedError
   116	    end
   117	
   118	    ##
   119	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   120	    def images
   121	      raise NotImplementedError
   122	    end
   123	
   124	    ##
   125	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   126	    def audio
   127	      raise NotImplementedError
   128	    end
   129	
   130	    ##
   131	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   132	    def moderations
   133	      raise NotImplementedError
   134	    end
   135	
   136	    ##
   137	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   138	    def responses
   139	      raise NotImplementedError
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    ##
   143	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   144	    def vector_stores
   145	      raise NotImplementedError
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    ##
   149	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   150	    def embed(input, model: nil, **params)
   151	      raise NotImplementedError
   152	    end
   153	
   154	    ##
   155	    # @return [String]
   156	    def assistant_role
   157	      "assistant"
   158	    end
   159	
   160	    ##
   161	    # Bedrock expects tool results as user messages containing
   162	    # `toolResult` content blocks rather than a distinct `tool` role.
   163	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
   164	    def tool_role
   165	      :user
   166	    end
   167	
   168	    ##
   169	    # @return [String]
   170	    def default_model
   171	      "deepseek.v3.2"
   172	    end
   173	
   174	    private
   175	
   176	    def headers
   177	      lock do
   178	        (@headers || {}).merge("Content-Type" => "application/json")
   179	      end
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    def credentials
   183	      LLM::Object.from(
   184	        access_key_id: @access_key_id,
   185	        secret_access_key: @secret_access_key,
   186	        aws_region: @aws_region,
   187	        host: @aws_host,
   188	        session_token: @session_token
   189	      )
   190	    end
   191	
   192	    def stream_parser
   193	      LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser
   194	    end
   195	
   196	    def error_handler
   197	      LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler
   198	    end
   199	
   200	    def stream_decoder
   201	      LLM::Bedrock::StreamDecoder
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   205	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
   206	      tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   207	      params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   208	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   209	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   210	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   211	    end
   212	
   213	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
   214	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   215	      model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
   216	      payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
   217	      body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
   218	      path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
   219	                    : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
   220	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
   221	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   222	      [req, messages, body]
   223	    end
   224	
   225	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   226	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   227	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
   228	      else
   229	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
   230	      end
   231	    end
   232	
   233	    def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
   234	      adapted = adapt(messages)
   235	      payload = {}
   236	      payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
   237	      payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
   238	      inference_config = {}
   239	      inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
   240	      inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
   241	      inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
   242	      inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
   243	      payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
   244	      payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
   245	      payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
   246	      additional = {}
   247	      top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
   248	      additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
   249	      payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
   250	      payload
   251	    end
   252	
   253	    def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
   254	      message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
   255	      value = message&.content || fallback
   256	      value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
   257	    end
   258	
   259	    def model_id_for(path)
   260	      path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model

     2	
     3	require_relative "openai" unless defined?(LLM::OpenAI)
     4	
     5	module LLM
     6	  ##
     7	  # The XAI class implements a provider for [xAI](https://docs.x.ai).
     8	  #
     9	  # @example
    10	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    11	  #   require "llm"
    12	  #

     3	class LLM::OpenAI
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  module RequestAdapter
     7	    require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
     8	    require_relative "request_adapter/respond"
     9	    require_relative "request_adapter/moderation"
    10	
    11	    ##
    12	    # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
    13	    #  The messages to adapt
    14	    # @param [Symbol] mode
    15	    #  The mode to adapt the messages for
    16	    # @return [Array<Hash>]
    17	    def adapt(messages, mode: :complete)
    18	      messages.filter_map do |message|
    19	        if mode == :complete
    20	          Completion.new(message).adapt
    21	        else
    22	          Respond.new(message).adapt
    23	        end
    24	      end
    25	    end
    26	
    27	    private
    28	
    29	    ##
    30	    # @param [Hash] params
    31	    # @return [Hash]
    32	    def adapt_schema(params)
    33	      return {} unless params and params[:schema]
    34	      schema = params.delete(:schema)
    35	      schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
    36	      {
    37	        response_format: {
    38	          type: "json_schema",
    39	          json_schema: {name: "JSONSchema", schema:}
    40	        }
    41	      }
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    ##
    45	    # @param [Hash] params
    46	    # @return [Hash]
    47	    def adapt_tools(tools)
    48	      if tools.nil? || tools.empty?
    49	        {}
    50	      else
    51	        {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
    52	      end
    53	    end
    54	  end
    55	end

     3	class LLM::Anthropic
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  module RequestAdapter
     7	    require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
     8	
     9	    ##
    10	    # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
    11	    #  The messages to adapt
    12	    # @return [Hash]
    13	    def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
    14	      payload = {messages: [], system: []}
    15	      messages.each do |message|
    16	        adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
    17	        next if adapted.nil?
    18	        if system?(message)
    19	          payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
    20	        else
    21	          payload[:messages] << adapted
    22	        end
    23	      end
    24	      payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
    25	      payload
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    private
    29	
    30	    ##
    31	    # @param [Hash] params
    32	    # @return [Hash]
    33	    def adapt_tools(tools)
    34	      return {} unless tools&.any?
    35	      {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
    36	    end
    37	
    38	    def system?(message)
    39	      if message.respond_to?(:system?)
    40	        message.system?
    41	      else
    42	        Hash === message and message[:role].to_s == "system"
    43	      end
    44	    end
    45	  end
    46	end

     4	  ##
     5	  # @private

     9	    ##

    26	      schema = params.delete(:schema)

    28	      {generationConfig: {response_mime_type: "application/json", response_schema: schema}}

     3	class LLM::Bedrock
     4	  ##
     5	  # Adapts llm.rb internal message format to Bedrock Converse API format.
     6	  #
     7	  # Bedrock Converse uses:
     8	  #   - system: [{text: "..."}]  (top-level, separate from messages)
     9	  #   - messages: [{role: "user"|"assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}]
    10	  #   - Content blocks: text, image, document, toolUse, toolResult
    11	  #   - toolConfig: {tools: [{toolSpec: {name:, description:, inputSchema: {json: ...}}}]}
    12	  #
    13	  # @api private
    14	  module RequestAdapter
    15	    ##
    16	    # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
    17	    # @return [Hash]
    18	    def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
    19	      payload = {messages: [], system: []}
    20	      messages.each do |message|
    21	        adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
    22	        next if adapted.nil?
    23	        if system?(message)
    24	          payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
    25	        else
    26	          payload[:messages] << adapted
    27	        end
    28	      end
    29	      payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
    30	      payload
    31	    end
    32	
    33	    private
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @param [Hash] params
    37	    # @return [Hash]
    38	    def adapt_schema(params)
    39	      return {} unless params&.key?(:schema)
    40	      schema = params.delete(:schema)
    41	      schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
    42	      cleaned = schema.respond_to?(:to_h) ? schema.to_h : schema
    43	      [:strict, "strict", :$schema, "$schema"].each { cleaned.delete(_1) }
    44	      {
    45	        outputConfig: {
    46	          textFormat: {
    47	            type: "json_schema",
    48	            structure: {
    49	              jsonSchema: {
    50	                name: "response",
    51	                schema: LLM.json.dump(cleaned)
    52	              }
    53	            }
    54	          }
    55	        }
    56	      }
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    ##
    60	    # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] tools
    61	    # @return [Hash]
    62	    def adapt_tools(tools)
    63	      return {} unless tools&.any?
    64	      {toolConfig: {tools: tools.map { |t| adapt_tool(t) }}}
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    ##
    68	    # @param [LLM::Function] tool
    69	    # @return [Hash]
    70	    def adapt_tool(tool)
    71	      function = tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
    72	      {
    73	        toolSpec: {
    74	          name: function.name,
    75	          description: function.description,
    76	          inputSchema: {
    77	            json: function.params || default_input_schema
    78	          }
    79	        }
    80	      }
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def default_input_schema
    84	      {"type" => "object", "properties" => {}, "required" => []}
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    def system?(message)
    88	      if message.respond_to?(:system?)
    89	        message.system?
    90	      else
    91	        Hash === message && message[:role].to_s == "system"
    92	      end
    93	    end
    94	  end
    95	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM::OpenAI::RequestAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class Completion
     7	    ##
     8	    # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
     9	    #  The message to format
    10	    def initialize(message)
    11	      @message = message
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Adapts the message for the OpenAI chat completions API
    16	    # @return [Hash]
    17	    def adapt
    18	      catch(:abort) do
    19	        if Hash === message
    20	          {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
    21	        elsif message.tool_call?
    22	          {role: message.role, content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls]}
    23	        else
    24	          adapt_message
    25	        end
    26	      end
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    private
    30	
    31	    def adapt_message
    32	      case content
    33	      when Array
    34	        adapt_array
    35	      else
    36	        {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def adapt_array
    41	      if content.empty?
    42	        nil
    43	      elsif returns.any?
    44	        returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
    45	      else
    46	        {role: message.role, content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }}
    47	      end
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    def adapt_content(content)
    51	      case content
    52	      when LLM::Object
    53	        adapt_object(content)
    54	      when String
    55	        [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
    56	      when LLM::Response
    57	        adapt_remote_file(content)
    58	      when LLM::Message
    59	        adapt_content(content.content)
    60	      when LLM::Function::Return
    61	        throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
    62	      else
    63	        prompt_error!(content)
    64	      end
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def adapt_object(object)
    68	      case object.kind
    69	      when :image_url
    70	        [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: object.value}}]
    71	      when :local_file
    72	        adapt_local_file(object.value)
    73	      when :remote_file
    74	        adapt_remote_file(object.value)
    75	      else
    76	        prompt_error!(object)
    77	      end
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    def adapt_local_file(file)
    81	      if file.image?
    82	        [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: file.to_data_uri}}]
    83	      else
    84	        [{type: :file, file: {filename: file.basename, file_data: file.to_data_uri}}]
    85	      end
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    def adapt_remote_file(file)
    89	      if file.file?
    90	        [{type: :file, file: {file_id: file.id}}]
    91	      else
    92	        prompt_error!(file)
    93	      end
    94	    end
    95	
    96	    def prompt_error!(content)
    97	      if LLM::Object === content
    98	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
    99	                                "supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
   100	      else
   101	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
   102	                                "is not supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
   103	      end
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    def message = @message
   107	    def content = message.content
   108	    def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
   109	  end
   110	end

     3	module LLM::Anthropic::RequestAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class Completion
     7	    ##
     8	    # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
     9	    #  The message to format
    10	    def initialize(message)
    11	      @message = message
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Adapts the message for the Anthropic chat completions API
    16	    # @return [Hash]
    17	    def adapt
    18	      catch(:abort) do
    19	        if Hash === message
    20	          {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
    21	        else
    22	          adapt_message
    23	        end
    24	      end
    25	    end
    26	
    27	    private
    28	
    29	    def adapt_message
    30	      if message.tool_call?
    31	        {role: message.role, content: adapt_tool_calls}
    32	      else
    33	        {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
    34	      end
    35	    end
    36	
    37	    def adapt_tool_calls
    38	      message.extra[:tool_calls].filter_map do |tool|
    39	        next unless tool[:id] && tool[:name]
    40	        {type: "tool_use", id: tool[:id], name: tool[:name], input: LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool[:arguments])}
    41	      end
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    ##
    45	    # @param [String, URI] content
    46	    #  The content to format
    47	    # @return [String, Hash]
    48	    #  The formatted content
    49	    def adapt_content(content)
    50	      case content
    51	      when Hash
    52	        content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : [content]
    53	      when Array
    54	        content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
    55	      when LLM::Object
    56	        adapt_object(content)
    57	      when String
    58	        [{type: :text, text: content}]
    59	      when LLM::Response
    60	        adapt_remote_file(content)
    61	      when LLM::Message
    62	        adapt_content(content.content)
    63	      when LLM::Function::Return
    64	        [{type: "tool_result", tool_use_id: content.id, content: [{type: :text, text: LLM.json.dump(content.value)}]}]
    65	      else
    66	        prompt_error!(content)
    67	      end
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    def adapt_object(object)
    71	      case object.kind
    72	      when :image_url
    73	        [{type: :image, source: {type: "url", url: object.value.to_s}}]
    74	      when :local_file
    75	        adapt_local_file(object.value)
    76	      when :remote_file
    77	        adapt_remote_file(object.value)
    78	      else
    79	        prompt_error!(content)
    80	      end
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def adapt_local_file(file)
    84	      if file.image?
    85	        [{type: :image, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
    86	      elsif file.pdf?
    87	        [{type: :document, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
    88	      else
    89	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
    90	                                "is not an image or PDF, and therefore not supported by the " \
    91	                                "Anthropic API"
    92	      end
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    def adapt_remote_file(file)
    96	      prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
    97	      [{type: file.file_type, source: {type: :file, file_id: file.id}}]
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    def prompt_error!(content)
   101	      if LLM::Object === content
   102	      else
   103	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
   104	                                "is not supported by the Anthropic API."
   105	      end
   106	    end
   107	
   108	    def message = @message
   109	    def content = message.content
   110	  end
   111	end

     3	module LLM::Google::RequestAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class Completion
     7	    ##
     8	    # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
     9	    #  The message to format
    10	    def initialize(message)
    11	      @message = message
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Adapts the message for the Gemini chat completions API
    16	    # @return [Hash]
    17	    def adapt
    18	      catch(:abort) do
    19	        if Hash === message
    20	          {role: message[:role], parts: adapt_content(message[:content])}
    21	        elsif message.tool_call?
    22	          {role: message.role, parts: message.extra.original_tool_calls}
    23	        else
    24	          {role: message.role, parts: adapt_content(message.content)}
    25	        end
    26	      end
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    def adapt_content(content)
    30	      case content
    31	      when Array
    32	        content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
    33	      when String
    34	        [{text: content}]
    35	      when LLM::Response
    36	        adapt_remote_file(content)
    37	      when LLM::Message
    38	        adapt_content(content.content)
    39	      when LLM::Function::Return
    40	        [{functionResponse: {name: content.name, response: adapt_function_response(content.value)}}]
    41	      when LLM::Object
    42	        adapt_object(content)
    43	      else
    44	        prompt_error!(content)
    45	      end
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    def adapt_object(object)
    49	      case object.kind
    50	      when :image_url
    51	        [{file_data: {file_uri: object.value.to_s}}]
    52	      when :local_file
    53	        file = object.value
    54	        [{inline_data: {mime_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
    55	      when :remote_file
    56	        adapt_remote_file(object.value)
    57	      else
    58	        prompt_error!(object)
    59	      end
    60	    end
    61	
    62	    def adapt_remote_file(file)
    63	      return prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
    64	      [{file_data: {mime_type: file.mime_type, file_uri: file.uri}}]
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def adapt_function_response(value)
    68	      Hash === value ? value : {result: value}
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def prompt_error!(object)
    72	      if LLM::Object === object
    73	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
    74	                                "supported by the Gemini API"
    75	      else
    76	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
    77	                                "is not supported by the Gemini API"
    78	      end
    79	    end
    80	
    81	    def message = @message
    82	    def content = message.content
    83	  end
    84	end
