     3	module LLM::Ollama::RequestAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class Completion
     7	    ##
     8	    # @param [LLM::Message] message
     9	    #  The message to format
    10	    def initialize(message)
    11	      @message = message
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Adapts the message for the Ollama chat completions API
    16	    # @return [Hash]
    17	    def adapt
    18	      catch(:abort) do
    19	        if Hash === message
    20	          {role: message[:role]}.merge(adapt_content(message[:content]))
    21	        else
    22	          adapt_message
    23	        end
    24	      end
    25	    end
    26	
    27	    private
    28	
    29	    def adapt_content(content)
    30	      case content
    31	      when String
    32	        {content:}
    33	      when LLM::Message
    34	        adapt_content(content.content)
    35	      when LLM::Function::Return
    36	        throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
    37	      when LLM::Object
    38	        adapt_object(content)
    39	      else
    40	        prompt_error!(content)
    41	      end
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    def adapt_message
    45	      case content
    46	      when Array
    47	        adapt_array
    48	      else
    49	        {role: message.role}.merge(adapt_content(content))
    50	      end
    51	    end
    52	
    53	    def adapt_array
    54	      if content.empty?
    55	        nil
    56	      elsif returns.any?
    57	        returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
    58	      else
    59	        content.flat_map { {role: message.role}.merge(adapt_content(_1)) }
    60	      end
    61	    end
    62	
    63	    def adapt_object(object)
    64	      case object.kind
    65	      when :local_file then adapt_local_file(object.value)
    66	      when :remote_file then prompt_error!(object)
    67	      when :image_url then prompt_error!(object)
    68	      else prompt_error!(object)
    69	      end
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    def adapt_local_file(file)
    73	      if file.image?
    74	        {content: "This message has an image associated with it", images: [file.to_b64]}
    75	      else
    76	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given local file (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
    77	                                "is not an image, and therefore not supported by the " \
    78	                                "Ollama API"
    79	      end
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    def prompt_error!(object)
    83	      if LLM::Object === object
    84	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
    85	                                "supported by the Ollama API"
    86	      else
    87	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
    88	                                "is not supported by the Ollama API"
    89	      end
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    def message = @message
    93	    def content = message.content
    94	    def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
    95	  end
    96	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM::DeepSeek::RequestAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class Completion
     7	    ##
     8	    # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
     9	    #  The message to format
    10	    def initialize(message)
    11	      @message = message
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Adapts the message for the DeepSeek chat completions API
    16	    # @return [Hash]
    17	    def adapt
    18	      catch(:abort) do
    19	        if Hash === message
    20	          {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
    21	        elsif message.tool_call?
    22	          wrap(content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls])
    23	        else
    24	          adapt_message
    25	        end
    26	      end
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    private
    30	
    31	    def adapt_content(content)
    32	      case content
    33	      when LLM::Object
    34	        adapt_object(content)
    35	      when String
    36	        [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
    37	      when LLM::Message
    38	        adapt_content(content.content)
    39	      when LLM::Function::Return
    40	        throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
    41	      else
    42	        prompt_error!(content)
    43	      end
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    def adapt_object(object)
    47	      case object.kind
    48	      when :image_url, :local_file, :remote_file
    49	        prompt_error!(object)
    50	      else
    51	        prompt_error!(object)
    52	      end
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    def adapt_message
    56	      case content
    57	      when Array
    58	        adapt_array
    59	      else
    60	        wrap(content: adapt_content(content))
    61	      end
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def adapt_array
    65	      if content.empty?
    66	        nil
    67	      elsif returns.any?
    68	        returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
    69	      else
    70	        wrap(content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) })
    71	      end
    72	    end
    73	
    74	    def prompt_error!(object)
    75	      if LLM::Object === object
    76	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{object.kind}' is not " \
    77	                                "supported by the DeepSeek API"
    78	      else
    79	        raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
    80	                                "is not supported by the DeepSeek API"
    81	      end
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    def wrap(content:, tool_calls: nil)
    85	      {
    86	        role: message.role,
    87	        content:,
    88	        tool_calls: tool_calls&.map { LLM::Object === _1 ? _1.to_h : _1 },
    89	        reasoning_content: message.reasoning_content
    90	      }.compact.then { preserve_nil_content(_1) }
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    def message = @message
    94	    def content = message.content
    95	    def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
    96	
    97	    def preserve_nil_content(hash)
    98	      hash[:content] = content if content.nil?
    99	      hash
   100	    end
   101	  end
   102	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM::Bedrock::RequestAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # Adapts a single message to Bedrock Converse content blocks.
     6	  #
     7	  # Bedrock Converse content blocks include:
     8	  #   - {text: "..."}
     9	  #   - {image: {format: "png", source: {bytes: "..."}}}
    10	  #   - {document: {format: "pdf", name: "...", source: {bytes: "..."}}}
    11	  #   - {toolUse: {toolUseId: "...", name: "...", input: {...}}}
    12	  #   - {toolResult: {toolUseId: "...", content: [{text: "..."}]}}
    13	  #
    14	  # @api private
    15	  class Completion
    16	    ##
    17	    # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
    18	    def initialize(message)
    19	      @message = message
    20	    end
    21	
    22	    ##
    23	    # Adapts the message for the Bedrock Converse API
    24	    # @return [Hash, nil]
    25	    def adapt
    26	      catch(:abort) do
    27	        if Hash === message
    28	          {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
    29	        else
    30	          adapt_message
    31	        end
    32	      end
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    private
    36	
    37	    def adapt_message
    38	      if message.tool_call?
    39	        blocks = [*adapt_tool_calls]
    40	        blocks.unshift(*adapt_content(content)) unless String === content && content.empty?
    41	        {role: "assistant", content: blocks}
    42	      else
    43	        {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
    44	      end
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    def adapt_tool_calls
    48	      message.extra[:tool_calls].filter_map do |tool|
    49	        next unless tool[:id] && tool[:name]
    50	        {
    51	          toolUse: {
    52	            toolUseId: tool[:id],
    53	            name: tool[:name],
    54	            input: parse_tool_input(tool[:arguments])
    55	          }
    56	        }
    57	      end
    58	    end
    59	
    60	    ##
    61	    # @param [String, Array, LLM::Object, LLM::Function::Return] content
    62	    # @return [Array<Hash>, nil]
    63	    def adapt_content(content)
    64	      case content
    65	      when Hash
    66	        content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : [content]
    67	      when Array
    68	        content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
    69	      when LLM::Object
    70	        adapt_object(content)
    71	      when String
    72	        [{text: content}]
    73	      when LLM::Response
    74	        adapt_remote_file(content)
    75	      when LLM::Message
    76	        adapt_content(content.content)
    77	      when LLM::Function::Return
    78	        [{toolResult: {toolUseId: content.id, content: [{text: LLM.json.dump(content.value)}]}}]
    79	      else
    80	        prompt_error!(content)
    81	      end
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    def adapt_object(object)
    85	      case object.kind
    86	      when :image_url
    87	        [{image: {format: detect_format(object.value.to_s),
    88	                  source: {url: object.value.to_s}}}]
    89	      when :local_file
    90	        adapt_local_file(object.value)
    91	      when :remote_file
    92	        adapt_remote_file(object.value)
    93	      else
    94	        prompt_error!(object)
    95	      end
    96	    end
    97	
    98	    def adapt_local_file(file)
    99	      if file.image?
   100	        [{image: {format: file.format,
   101	                  source: {bytes: file.to_b64}}}]
   102	      elsif file.pdf?
   103	        name = sanitize_name(file.basename)
   104	        [{document: {format: "pdf", name:,
   105	                     source: {bytes: file.to_b64}}}]
   106	      else
   107	        raise LLM::PromptError,
   108	              "The #{file.class} is not an image or PDF, " \
   109	              "and not supported by the Bedrock API"
   110	      end
   111	    end
   112	
   113	    def adapt_remote_file(file)
   114	      prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
   115	      [{file.file_type => {source: {file_id: file.id}}}]
   116	    end
   117	
   118	    def detect_format(url)
   119	      case url
   120	      when /\.png/i then "png"
   121	      when /\.jpe?g/i then "jpeg"
   122	      when /\.gif/i then "gif"
   123	      when /\.webp/i then "webp"
   124	      else "png"
   125	      end
   126	    end
   127	
   128	    def sanitize_name(name)
   129	      name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/, "_")
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    def parse_tool_input(input)
   133	      case input
   134	      when Hash then input
   135	      when String
   136	        parsed = LLM.json.load(input)
   137	        Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
   138	      when nil then {}
   139	      else input.respond_to?(:to_h) ? input.to_h : {}
   140	      end
   141	    rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
   142	      {}
   143	    end
   144	
   145	    def prompt_error!(content)
   146	      raise LLM::PromptError,
   147	            "#{content.class} is not supported by the Bedrock API"
   148	    end
   149	
   150	    def message = @message
   151	    def content = message.content
   152	  end
   153	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::Transport
     4	  ##
     5	  # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
     6	  #
     7	  # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
     8	  # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
     9	  #
    10	  # @api private
    11	  module Execution
    12	    private
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Executes a HTTP request
    16	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
    17	    #  The request to send
    18	    # @param [Proc] b
    19	    #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
    20	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    21	    #  The response from the server
    22	    # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
    23	    #  When authentication fails
    24	    # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
    25	    #  When the rate limit is exceeded
    26	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    27	    #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
    28	    # @raise [SystemCallError]
    29	    #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
    30	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    31	    def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
    32	      stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
    33	      owner = transport.request_owner
    34	      tracer = self.tracer
    35	      span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
    36	      res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
    37	      res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    38	      [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
    39	    rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
    40	      raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
    41	      raise
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    ##
    45	    # Handles the response from a request
    46	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
    47	    #  The response to handle
    48	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    49	    #  The span
    50	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    51	    def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
    52	      res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
    53	      res
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    ##
    57	    # Parse a HTTP response
    58	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
    59	    # @return [LLM::Object, String]
    60	    def parse_response(res)
    61	      case res["content-type"]
    62	      when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
    63	      else res.body
    64	      end
    65	    end
    66	  end
    67	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The superclass of all LLM errors
     6	  class Error < RuntimeError
     7	    ##
     8	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response, nil]
     9	    #  Returns the response associated with an error, or nil
    10	    attr_accessor :response
    11	
    12	    def initialize(...)
    13	      block_given? ? yield(self) : nil
    14	      super
    15	    end
    16	
    17	    def message
    18	      if response
    19	        [super, response.body].join("\n")
    20	      else
    21	        super
    22	      end
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	
    26	  ##
    27	  # HTTPUnauthorized
    28	  UnauthorizedError = Class.new(Error)
    29	
    30	  ##
    31	  # HTTPTooManyRequests
    32	  RateLimitError = Class.new(Error)
    33	
    34	  ##
    35	  # HTTPServerError
    36	  ServerError = Class.new(Error)
    37	
    38	  ##
    39	  # HTTPNotFound
    40	  NotFoundError = Class.new(Error)
    41	
    42	  ##
    43	  # When an given an input object that is not understood
    44	  FormatError = Class.new(Error)
    45	
    46	  ##
    47	  # When given a prompt object that is not understood
    48	  PromptError = Class.new(FormatError)
    49	
    50	  ##
    51	  # When given an invalid request
    52	  InvalidRequestError = Class.new(Error)
    53	
    54	  ##
    55	  # When the context window is exceeded
    56	  ContextWindowError = Class.new(InvalidRequestError)
    57	
    58	  ##
    59	  # When stuck in a tool call loop
    60	  ToolLoopError = Class.new(Error)
    61	
    62	  ##
    63	  # When a guard blocks pending tool execution
    64	  GuardError = Class.new(Error)
    65	
    66	  ##
    67	  # When a request is interrupted
    68	  Interrupt = Class.new(Error)
    69	
    70	  ##
    71	  # When a concurrency strategy cannot execute a given tool
    72	  RactorError = Class.new(Error)
    73	
    74	  ##
    75	  # When a tool call cannot be mapped to a local tool
    76	  NoSuchToolError = Class.new(Error)
    77	
    78	  ##
    79	  # When {LLM::Registry} can't map a model
    80	  NoSuchModelError = Class.new(Error)
    81	
    82	  ##
    83	  # When {LLM::Registry} can't map a registry
    84	  NoSuchRegistryError = Class.new(Error)
    85	
    86	  ##
    87	  # When an optional runtime dependency cannot be required
    88	  LoadError = Class.new(Error)
    89	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # {LLM::Response LLM::Response} is the normalized base shape for
     6	  # provider and endpoint responses in llm.rb.
     7	  #
     8	  # Provider calls return an instance of this class, then extend it
     9	  # with provider-, endpoint-, or context-specific modules so response
    10	  # handling can share one common surface without flattening away
    11	  # specialized behavior.
    12	  #
    13	  # The normalized response keeps the transport response available
    14	  # through {#res}. When the default net/http transport is in use,
    15	  # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP
    16	  # LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP} keeps the
    17	  # original {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} available through
    18	  # its own {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP#res #res}.
    19	  class Response
    20	    require "json"
    21	
    22	    ##
    23	    # Returns the HTTP response
    24	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    25	    attr_reader :res
    26	
    27	    ##
    28	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
    29	    #  HTTP response
    30	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    31	    #  Returns an instance of LLM::Response
    32	    def initialize(res)
    33	      @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    ##
    37	    # Returns the response body
    38	    # @return [LLM::Object, String]
    39	    #  Returns an LLM::Object when the response body is JSON,
    40	    #  otherwise returns a raw string.
    41	    def body
    42	      @res.body
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    ##
    46	    # Returns an inspection of the response object
    47	    # @return [String]
    48	    def inspect
    49	      "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @body=#{body.inspect} @res=#{@res.inspect}>"
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    ##
    53	    # Returns true if the response is successful
    54	    # @return [Boolean]
    55	    def ok?
    56	      @res.success?
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    ##
    60	    # Returns true if the response is from the Files API
    61	    # @return [Boolean]
    62	    def file?
    63	      false
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    private
    67	
    68	    def method_missing(m, *args, **kwargs, &b)
    69	      if LLM::Object === body
    70	        body.respond_to?(m) ? body[m.to_s] : super
    71	      else
    72	        super
    73	      end
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def respond_to_missing?(m, include_private = false)
    77	      if LLM::Object === body
    78	        body.respond_to?(m)
    79	      else
    80	        false
    81	      end
    82	    end
    83	  end
    84	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  class Message
     5	    ##
     6	    # Returns the role of the message
     7	    # @return [Symbol]
     8	    attr_reader :role
     9	
    10	    ##
    11	    # Returns the content of the message
    12	    # @return [String]
    13	    attr_reader :content
    14	
    15	    ##
    16	    # Returns extra context associated with the message
    17	    # @return [Hash]
    18	    attr_reader :extra
    19	
    20	    ##
    21	    # Returns a new message
    22	    # @param [Symbol] role
    23	    # @param [String] content
    24	    # @param [Hash] extra
    25	    # @return [LLM::Message]
    26	    def initialize(role, content, extra = {})
    27	      @role = role.to_s
    28	      @content = content
    29	      @extra = LLM::Object.from(extra)
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    ##
    33	    # Returns a Hash representation of the message.
    34	    # @return [Hash]
    35	    def to_h
    36	      {
    37	        role:,
    38	        content:,
    39	        reasoning_content:,
    40	        compaction: extra.compaction,
    41	        tools: extra.tool_calls&.map { LLM::Object === _1 ? _1.to_h : _1 },
    42	        usage:,
    43	        original_tool_calls: extra.original_tool_calls
    44	      }.compact.then { preserve_nil_content(_1) }
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    ##
    48	    # @return [String]
    49	    def to_json(...)
    50	      LLM.json.dump(to_h, ...)
    51	    end
    52	
    53	    ##
    54	    # Returns true when two objects have the same role and content
    55	    # @param [Object] other
    56	    #  The other object to compare
    57	    # @return [Boolean]
    58	    def ==(other)
    59	      if other.respond_to?(:to_h)
    60	        to_h == other.to_h
    61	      else
    62	        false
    63	      end
    64	    end
    65	    alias_method :eql?, :==
    66	
    67	    ##
    68	    # Try to parse JSON content
    69	    # @return [Hash]
    70	    #  Returns the parsed content as a Hash
    71	    def content!
    72	      LLM.json.load(content)
    73	    end
    74	
    75	    ##
    76	    # Returns reasoning content associated with the message
    77	    # @return [String, nil]
    78	    def reasoning_content
    79	      extra.reasoning_content
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    ##
    83	    # Returns true when a message was created by context compaction
    84	    # @return [Boolean]
    85	    def compaction?
    86	      !!extra.compaction
    87	    end
    88	
    89	    ##
    90	    # Returns true when a message contains an image URL
    91	    # @return [Boolean]
    92	    def image_url?
    93	      image_urls.any?
    94	    end
    95	
    96	    ##
    97	    # Returns image URL content items from the message
    98	    # @return [Array<LLM::Object>]
    99	    def image_urls
   100	      content_items.select { LLM::Object === _1 && _1.kind == :image_url }
   101	    end
   102	
   103	    ##
   104	    # Returns true when a message contains a local or remote file
   105	    # @return [Boolean]
   106	    def file?
   107	      files.any?
   108	    end
   109	
   110	    ##
   111	    # Returns local and remote file content items from the message
   112	    # @return [Array<LLM::Object>]
   113	    def files
   114	      content_items.select do
   115	        LLM::Object === _1 && [:local_file, :remote_file].include?(_1.kind)
   116	      end
   117	    end
   118	
   119	    ##
   120	    # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
   121	    def functions
   122	      @functions ||= tool_calls.filter_map do |fn|
   123	        function = available_tools.find { _1.name.to_s == fn["name"] } || next
   124	        function = function.dup
   125	        function.tap { _1.id = fn.id }
   126	        function.tap { _1.arguments = fn.arguments }
   127	      end
   128	    end
   129	
   130	    ##
   131	    # Returns true when the message is an assistant message
   132	    # @return [Boolean]
   133	    def assistant?
   134	      role == "assistant" || role == "model"
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    ##
   138	    # Returns true when the message is a system message
   139	    # @return [Boolean]
   140	    def system?
   141	      role == "system"
   142	    end
   143	
   144	    ##
   145	    # Returns true when the message is a user message
   146	    # @return [Boolean]
   147	    def user?
   148	      role == "user"
   149	    end
   150	
   151	    ##
   152	    # @return [Boolean]
   153	    #  Returns true when the message requests a function call
   154	    def tool_call?
   155	      tool_calls.any?
   156	    end
   157	
   158	    ##
   159	    # @return [Boolean]
   160	    #  Returns true when the message represents a function return
   161	    def tool_return?
   162	      LLM::Function::Return === content ||
   163	        [*content].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
   164	    end
   165	
   166	    ##
   167	    # @note
   168	    #  This method returns a response for assistant messages,
   169	    #  and it returns nil for non-assistant messages
   170	    # @return [LLM::Response, nil]
   171	    #  Returns the response associated with the message, or nil
   172	    def response
   173	      extra.response
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    ##
   177	    # @note
   178	    #  This method might return annotations for assistant messages,
   179	    #  and it returns an empty array for non-assistant messages
   180	    # Returns annotations associated with the message
   181	    # @return [Array<LLM::Object>]
   182	    def annotations
   183	      @annotations ||= LLM::Object.from(extra.annotations || [])
   184	    end
   185	
   186	    ##
   187	    # @note
   188	    #  This method returns token usage for assistant messages,
   189	    #  and it returns nil for non-assistant messages
   190	    # Returns token usage statistics
   191	    # @return [LLM::Object, nil]
   192	    def usage
   193	      @usage ||= extra.usage || response&.usage
   194	    end
   195	    alias_method :token_usage, :usage
   196	
   197	    ##
   198	    # @return [String, nil]
   199	    #  Returns the model associated with a message
   200	    def model
   201	      response&.model
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    ##
   205	    # Returns a string representation of the message
   206	    # @return [String]
   207	    def inspect
   208	      "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} " \
   209	      "tool_call=#{tool_calls.any?} role=#{role.inspect} " \
   210	      "content=#{content.inspect} reasoning_content=#{reasoning_content.inspect}>"
   211	    end
   212	
   213	    private
   214	
   215	    def preserve_nil_content(hash)
   216	      hash[:content] = content if content.nil?
   217	      hash
   218	    end
   219	
   220	    def tool_calls
   221	      @tool_calls ||= LLM::Object.from(extra.tool_calls || [])
   222	    end
   223	
   224	    def available_tools
   225	      tools = extra.tools || response&.__tools__ || []
   226	      tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:function) ? _1.function : _1 }
   227	    end
   228	
   229	    def content_items
   230	      Array(content)
   231	    end
   232	  end
   233	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The {LLM::Stream LLM::Stream} class provides the callback interface for
     6	  # streamed model output in llm.rb.
     7	  #
     8	  # A stream object can be an instance of {LLM::Stream LLM::Stream} or a
     9	  # subclass that overrides the callbacks it needs. For basic streaming,
    10	  # llm.rb also accepts any object that implements `#<<`. {#queue} provides
    11	  # a small helper for collecting asynchronous tool work started from a
    12	  # callback.
    13	  #
    14	  # @note The `on_*` callbacks run inline with the streaming parser. They
    15	  #   therefore block streaming progress and should generally return as
    16	  #   quickly as possible.
    17	  #
    18	  # The most common callback is {#on_content}, which also maps to {#<<}.
    19	  # Providers may also call {#on_reasoning_content} and {#on_tool_call} when
    20	  # that data is available. Runtime features such as context compaction may
    21	  # also emit lifecycle callbacks like {#on_transform} or {#on_compaction}.
    22	  class Stream
    23	    require_relative "stream/queue"
    24	
    25	    ##
    26	    # Returns extra context associated with the current streamed request.
    27	    # @return [Hash]
    28	    def extra
    29	      @extra ||= LLM::Object.from({})
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    ##
    33	    # Returns the current context, if one was attached to the stream.
    34	    # @return [LLM::Context, nil]
    35	    def ctx
    36	      extra[:ctx]
    37	    end
    38	
    39	    ##
    40	    # Returns a lazily-initialized queue for tool results or spawned work.
    41	    # @return [LLM::Stream::Queue]
    42	    def queue
    43	      @queue ||= Queue.new(self)
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    ##
    47	    # Waits for queued tool work to finish and returns function results.
    48	    # Any passed arguments are ignored because queued work is waited according
    49	    # to the actual task types already present in the queue.
    50	    # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
    51	    def wait(*)
    52	      queue.wait
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    # @group Public callbacks
    56	
    57	    ##
    58	    # Called when visible assistant output is streamed.
    59	    # @param [String] content
    60	    #  A chunk of assistant-visible text.
    61	    # @return [nil]
    62	    def on_content(content)
    63	      nil
    64	    end
    65	    alias_method :<<, :on_content
    66	
    67	    ##
    68	    # Called when reasoning output is streamed separately from visible content.
    69	    # @param [String] content
    70	    #  A chunk of reasoning text.
    71	    # @return [nil]
    72	    def on_reasoning_content(content)
    73	      nil
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    ##
    77	    # Called when a streamed tool call has been fully constructed.
    78	    # @note A stream implementation may start tool execution here, for
    79	    #   example by pushing `ctx.spawn(tool, :thread)`,
    80	    #   `ctx.spawn(tool, :fiber)`, or `ctx.spawn(tool, :task)` onto {#queue}.
    81	    #   Mixed strategies can also be selected per tool, such as
    82	    #   `tool.mcp? ? ctx.spawn(tool, :task) : ctx.spawn(tool, :ractor)`.
    83	    #   When a streamed tool cannot be resolved, `error` is passed as an
    84	    #   {LLM::Function::Return}. It can be sent back to the model, allowing
    85	    #   the tool-call path to recover and the session to continue. Streamed
    86	    #   tool resolution now prefers the current request tools, so
    87	    #   {LLM.function}, MCP tools, bound tool instances, and normal
    88	    #   {LLM::Tool LLM::Tool} classes can all resolve through the same
    89	    #   request-local path. The current `:ractor` mode is for class-based
    90	    #   tools and does not support MCP tools.
    91	    # @param [LLM::Function] tool
    92	    #  The parsed tool call.
    93	    # @param [LLM::Function::Return, nil] error
    94	    #  An in-band tool error for unresolved tool calls.
    95	    # @return [nil]
    96	    def on_tool_call(tool, error)
    97	      nil
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    ##
   101	    # Called when queued streamed tool work returns.
   102	    # @note This callback runs when {#wait} resolves work that was queued from
   103	    #   {#on_tool_call}, such as values returned by `ctx.spawn(tool, :thread)`,
   104	    #   `ctx.spawn(tool, :fiber)`, or `ctx.spawn(tool, :task)`.
   105	    # @param [LLM::Function] tool
   106	    #  The tool that returned.
   107	    # @param [LLM::Function::Return] result
   108	    #  The completed tool return.
   109	    # @return [nil]
   110	    def on_tool_return(tool, result)
   111	      nil
   112	    end
   113	
   114	    ##
   115	    # Called before a context transformer rewrites a prompt.
   116	    # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
   117	    # @param [#call] transformer
   118	    # @return [nil]
   119	    def on_transform(ctx, transformer)
   120	      nil
   121	    end
   122	
   123	    ##
   124	    # Called after a context transformer finishes rewriting a prompt.
   125	    # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
   126	    # @param [#call] transformer
   127	    # @return [nil]
   128	    def on_transform_finish(ctx, transformer)
   129	      nil
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    ##
   133	    # Called before a context compaction starts.
   134	    # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
   135	    # @param [LLM::Compactor] compactor
   136	    # @return [nil]
   137	    def on_compaction(ctx, compactor)
   138	      nil
   139	    end
   140	
   141	    ##
   142	    # Called after a context compaction finishes.
   143	    # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
   144	    # @param [LLM::Compactor] compactor
   145	    # @return [nil]
   146	    def on_compaction_finish(ctx, compactor)
   147	      nil
   148	    end
   149	
   150	    # @endgroup
   151	
   152	    # @group Finders
   153	
   154	    ##
   155	    # Resolves a streamed tool call against the current request tools first,
   156	    # then falls back to the global function registry.
   157	    # @param [String] name
   158	    # @return [LLM::Function, nil]
   159	    def __find__(name)
   160	      tools = extra[:tools] || ctx&.params&.dig(:tools) || []
   161	      tool = tools.find do
   162	        candidate = _1.respond_to?(:function) ? _1.function.name : _1.name
   163	        candidate.to_s == name.to_s
   164	      end
   165	      if tool
   166	        tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
   167	      else
   168	        LLM::Function.find_by_name(name)
   169	      end
   170	    end
   171	
   172	    # @endgroup
   173	  end
   174	end

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'for f in lib/llm/providers/{openai,anthropic,google,ollama,bedrock}/response_adapter.rb lib/llm/providers/{openai,anthropic,google,ollama,bedrock}/response_adapter/completion.rb lib/llm/providers/{openai,google,ollama}/response_adapter/embedding.rb lib/llm/providers/{openai,anthropic,google,ollama,bedrock}/response_adapter/models.rb; do [ -f "$f" ] && nl -ba "$f" | sed -n '"'1,260p'; done"

     3	class LLM::OpenAI
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  module ResponseAdapter
     7	    require_relative "response_adapter/audio"
     8	    require_relative "response_adapter/completion"
     9	    require_relative "response_adapter/embedding"
    10	    require_relative "response_adapter/enumerable"
    11	    require_relative "response_adapter/file"
    12	    require_relative "response_adapter/image"
    13	    require_relative "response_adapter/moderations"
    14	    require_relative "response_adapter/models"
    15	    require_relative "response_adapter/responds"
    16	    require_relative "response_adapter/web_search"
    17	
    18	    module_function
    19	
    20	    ##
    21	    # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    22	    # @param [Symbol] type
    23	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    24	    def adapt(res, type:)
    25	      response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
    26	      adapter = select(type)
    27	      response.extend(adapter)
    28	    end
    29	
    30	    ##
    31	    # @api private
    32	    def select(type)
    33	      case type
    34	      when :audio then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Audio
    35	      when :completion then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Completion
    36	      when :embedding then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Embedding
    37	      when :enumerable then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Enumerable
    38	      when :file then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::File
    39	      when :image then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Image
    40	      when :moderations then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Moderations
    41	      when :models then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Models
    42	      when :responds then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Responds
    43	      when :web_search then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::WebSearch
    44	      else
    45	        raise ArgumentError, "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
    46	      end
    47	    end
    48	  end
    49	end

     4	  ##
     5	  # Adapts Bedrock Converse API responses to llm.rb's Response format.
     6	  #
     7	  # Bedrock Converse returns:
     8	  #   {
     9	  #     output: {message: {role: "assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}},
    10	  #     usage: {inputTokens: N, outputTokens: N},
    11	  #     modelId: "anthropic.claude-...",
    12	  #     stopReason: "end_turn" | "tool_use" | "max_tokens" | ...
    13	  #   }
    14	  #
    15	  # @api private
    16	  module ResponseAdapter
    17	    module_function
    18	
    19	    ##
    20	    # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    21	    # @param [Symbol] type
    22	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    23	    def adapt(res, type:)
    24	      response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
    25	      response.extend(select(type))
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    ##
    29	    # @api private
    30	    def select(type)
    31	      case type
    32	      when :completion then LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter::Completion
    33	      when :models then LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter::Models
    34	      else
    35	        raise ArgumentError,
    36	              "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	  end
    40	end

     3	module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
     4	  module Completion
     5	    ##
     6	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
     7	    def messages
     8	      body.choices.map.with_index do |choice, index|
     9	        message = choice.message
    10	        extra = {
    11	          index:, response: self,
    12	          logprobs: choice.logprobs,
    13	          reasoning_content: message.reasoning_content,
    14	          tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(message.tool_calls),
    15	          original_tool_calls: message.tool_calls
    16	        }
    17	        LLM::Message.new(message.role, message.content, extra)
    18	      end
    19	    end
    20	    alias_method :choices, :messages
    21	
    22	    ##
    23	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
    24	    def input_tokens
    25	      body.usage&.prompt_tokens || 0
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    ##
    29	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)
    30	    def output_tokens
    31	      body.usage&.completion_tokens || 0
    32	    end
    33	
    34	    ##
    35	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_tokens)
    36	    def reasoning_tokens
    37	      body
    38	        .usage
    39	        &.completion_tokens_details
    40	        &.reasoning_tokens || 0
    41	    end
    42	
    43	    ##
    44	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_audio_tokens)
    45	    def input_audio_tokens
    46	      body
    47	        .usage
    48	        &.prompt_tokens_details
    49	        &.audio_tokens || 0
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    ##
    53	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_audio_tokens)
    54	    def output_audio_tokens
    55	      body
    56	        .usage
    57	        &.completion_tokens_details
    58	        &.audio_tokens || 0
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    ##
    62	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_image_tokens)
    63	    def input_image_tokens
    64	      body
    65	        .usage
    66	        &.prompt_tokens_details

     4	  ##
     5	  # Adapts Bedrock Converse API completion responses.
     6	  #
     7	  # The Bedrock Converse response body looks like:
     8	  #   {
     9	  #     "output" => {"message" => {
    10	  #       "role" => "assistant",
    11	  #       "content" => [{"text" => "..."}, {"toolUse" => {...}}]
    12	  #     }},
    13	  #     "usage" => {"inputTokens" => N, "outputTokens" => N},
    14	  #     "modelId" => "anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0",
    15	  #     "stopReason" => "end_turn"
    16	  #   }
    17	  module Completion
    18	    ##
    19	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
    20	    def messages
    21	      source = texts.empty? && tools.any? ? [{"text" => ""}] : texts
    22	      source.map.with_index do |choice, index|
    23	        extra = {
    24	          index:, response: self,
    25	          reasoning_content:,
    26	          tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(tools),
    27	          original_tool_calls: tools
    28	        }
    29	        LLM::Message.new(role, choice["text"], extra)
    30	      end
    31	    end
    32	    alias_method :choices, :messages
    33	
    34	    ##
    35	    # Returns the Bedrock request id when present.
    36	    # @return [String, nil]
    37	    def id
    38	      res["x-amzn-requestid"] || res["x-amzn-request-id"]
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    ##
    42	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
    43	    def input_tokens
    44	      body.usage&.inputTokens || 0
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    ##
    48	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)
    49	    def output_tokens
    50	      body.usage&.outputTokens || 0
    51	    end
    52	
    53	    ##
    54	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_tokens)
    55	    def reasoning_tokens
    56	      0
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    ##
    60	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_audio_tokens)
    61	    def input_audio_tokens
    62	      super
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    ##
    66	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_audio_tokens)
    67	    def output_audio_tokens
    68	      super
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    ##
    72	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_image_tokens)
    73	    def input_image_tokens
    74	      super
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    ##
    78	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_read_tokens)
    79	    def cache_read_tokens
    80	      0
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    ##
    84	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_write_tokens)
    85	    def cache_write_tokens
    86	      0
    87	    end
    88	
    89	    ##
    90	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#total_tokens)
    91	    def total_tokens
    92	      input_tokens + output_tokens
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    ##
    96	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#usage)
    97	    def usage
    98	      super
    99	    end
   100	
   101	    ##
   102	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#model)
   103	    def model
   104	      body.modelId
   105	    end
   106	
   107	    ##
   108	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content)
   109	    def content
   110	      super
   111	    end
   112	
   113	    ##
   114	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_content)
   115	    def reasoning_content
   116	      @reasoning_content ||= begin
   117	        text = parts.filter_map { _1.dig("reasoningContent", "text") }.join
   118	        text.empty? ? nil : text
   119	      end
   120	    end
   121	
   122	    ##
   123	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content!)
   124	    def content!
   125	      super
   126	    end
   127	
   128	    private
   129	
   130	    def adapt_tool_calls(tools)
   131	      (tools || []).filter_map do |tool|
   132	        next unless tool["toolUse"]
   133	        {
   134	          id: tool["toolUse"]["toolUseId"],
   135	          name: tool["toolUse"]["name"],
   136	          arguments: parse_tool_input(tool["toolUse"]["input"])
   137	        }
   138	      end
   139	    end
   140	
   141	    def parse_tool_input(input)
   142	      case input
   143	      when Hash then input
   144	      when String
   145	        parsed = LLM.json.load(input)
   146	        Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
   147	      when nil then {}
   148	      else input.respond_to?(:to_h) ? input.to_h : {}
   149	      end
   150	    rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
   151	      {}
   152	    end
   153	
   154	    def parts
   155	      raw = body.output&.message&.content || []
   156	      raw.is_a?(Array) ? raw : [raw].compact
   157	    end
   158	
   159	    def texts
   160	      @texts ||= parts.select { |b| b["text"] }
   161	    end
   162	
   163	    def tools
   164	      @tools ||= parts.select { |b| b["toolUse"] }
   165	    end
   166	
   167	    def role
   168	      body.output&.message&.role || "assistant"
   169	    end
   170	
   171	    include LLM::Contract::Completion
   172	  end
   173	end

     3	module LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter
     4	  ##
     5	  # Adapts Bedrock ListFoundationModels API responses to llm.rb's
     6	  # normalized model collection format.
     7	  #
     8	  # The Bedrock ListFoundationModels response looks like:
     9	  #   {
    10	  #     "modelSummaries": [{
    11	  #       "modelId": "anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0",
    12	  #       "modelName": "Claude Sonnet 4",
    13	  #       "providerName": "Anthropic",
    14	  #       "inputModalities": ["TEXT", "IMAGE"],
    15	  #       "outputModalities": ["TEXT"],
    16	  #       ...
    17	  #     }]
    18	  #   }
    19	  module Models
    20	    include LLM::Model::Collection
    21	
    22	    private
    23	
    24	    def raw_models
    25	      (body.modelSummaries || []).map do |summary|
    26	        LLM::Object.from({
    27	          id: summary.modelId,
    28	          name: summary.modelName,
    29	          provider_name: summary.providerName
    30	        })
    31	      end
    32	    end
    33	  end
    34	end

     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Google class implements a provider for
     6	  # [Gemini](https://ai.google.dev/). The Google provider
     7	  # can accept multiple inputs (text, images, audio, and video).
     8	  # The inputs can be provided inline via the prompt for files
     9	  # under 20MB or via the Gemini Files API for files
    10	  # that are over 20MB.
    11	  #
    12	  # @example
    13	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    14	  #   require "llm"
    15	  #
    16	  #   llm = LLM.google(key: ENV["KEY"])
    17	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    18	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    19	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    20	  class Google < Provider
    21	    require_relative "google/utils"
    22	    require_relative "google/error_handler"
    23	    require_relative "google/request_adapter"
    24	    require_relative "google/response_adapter"
    25	    require_relative "google/stream_parser"
    26	    require_relative "google/models"
    27	    require_relative "google/images"
    28	    require_relative "google/audio"
    29	    require_relative "google/files"
    30	
    31	    include RequestAdapter
    32	    extend Utils
    33	
    34	    HOST = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
    35	
    36	    ##
    37	    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    38	    def initialize(**)
    39	      super(host: HOST, **)
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    ##
    43	    # @return [Symbol]
    44	    #  Returns the provider's name
    45	    def name
    46	      :google
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    ##
    50	    # Provides an embedding
    51	    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    52	    # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    53	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    54	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    55	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    56	    def embed(input, model: "gemini-embedding-001", **params)
    57	      model = model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
    58	      path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", "embedContent?key=#{@key}"].join(":")
    59	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
    60	      req.body = LLM.json.dump({content: {parts: [{text: input}]}})
    61	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
    62	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
    63	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
    64	      res
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    ##
    68	    # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
    69	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content#v1beta.models.generateContent Gemini docs
    70	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    71	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    72	    # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    73	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    74	    # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
    75	    #  When given an object a provider does not understand
    76	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    77	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    78	      params, stream, tools, role, model = normalize_complete_params(params)
    79	      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
    80	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model:)
    81	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    82	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    83	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model:, res:, span:)
    84	      res
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    ##
    88	    # Provides an interface to Gemini's audio API
    89	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/audio Gemini docs
    90	    # @return [LLM::Google::Audio]
    91	    def audio
    92	      LLM::Google::Audio.new(self)
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    ##
    96	    # Provides an interface to Gemini's image generation API
    97	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-generation Gemini docs
    98	    # @return [see LLM::Google::Images]
    99	    def images
   100	      LLM::Google::Images.new(self)
   101	    end
   102	
   103	    ##
   104	    # Provides an interface to Gemini's file management API
   105	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/files Gemini docs
   106	    # @return [LLM::Google::Files]
   107	    def files
   108	      LLM::Google::Files.new(self)
   109	    end
   110	
   111	    ##
   112	    # Provides an interface to Gemini's models API
   113	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models Gemini docs
   114	    # @return [LLM::Google::Models]
   115	    def models
   116	      LLM::Google::Models.new(self)
   117	    end
   118	
   119	    ##
   120	    # Returns the default model for chat completions
   121	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models#gemini-2.5-flash gemini-2.5-flash
   122	    # @return [String]
   123	    def default_model
   124	      "gemini-2.5-flash"
   125	    end
   126	
   127	    ##
   128	    # @note
   129	    #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
   130	    #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
   131	    #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
   132	    # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/google-search Gemini docs
   133	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
   134	    def server_tools
   135	      {
   136	        google_search: server_tool(:google_search),
   137	        code_execution: server_tool(:code_execution),
   138	        url_context: server_tool(:url_context)
   139	      }
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    ##
   143	    # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
   144	    # Google Search tool.
   145	    # @param query [String] The search query.
   146	    # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
   147	    def web_search(query:)
   148	      ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:google_search]]), type: :web_search)
   149	    end
   150	
   151	    ##
   152	    # @return [Symbol]
   153	    #  Returns the providers user role
   154	    def user_role
   155	      :user
   156	    end
   157	
   158	    ##
   159	    # @return [Symbol]
   160	    #  Returns the providers system role
   161	    def system_role
   162	      :user
   163	    end
   164	
   165	    ##
   166	    # @return [Symbol]
   167	    # Returns the providers developer role
   168	    def developer_role
   169	      :user
   170	    end
   171	
   172	    ##
   173	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
   174	    def assistant_role
   175	      "model"
   176	    end
   177	
   178	    private
   179	
   180	    def headers
   181	      lock do
   182	        (@headers || {}).merge(
   183	          "Content-Type" => "application/json"
   184	        )
   185	      end
   186	    end
   187	
   188	    def stream_parser
   189	      LLM::Google::StreamParser
   190	    end
   191	
   192	    def error_handler
   193	      LLM::Google::ErrorHandler
   194	    end
   195	
   196	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   197	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
   198	      tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   199	      params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   200	      role, model, stream = [:role, :model, :stream].map { params.delete(_1) }
   201	      [params, stream, tools, role, model]
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
   205	      action = stream ? "streamGenerateContent?key=#{@key}&alt=sse" : "generateContent?key=#{@key}"
   206	      model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
   207	      path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", action].join(":")
   208	      req  = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
   209	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   210	      body = LLM.json.dump({contents: adapt(messages)}.merge!(params))
   211	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   212	      req
   213	    end
   214	
   215	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   216	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   217	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
   218	      else
   219	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
   220	      end
   221	    end
   222	  end
   223	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
     6	  # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
     7	  # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
     8	  # that can process both images and text.
     9	  #
    10	  # @example
    11	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    12	  #   require "llm"
    13	  #
    14	  #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
    15	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
    16	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    17	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    18	  class Ollama < Provider
    19	    require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
    20	    require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
    21	    require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
    22	    require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
    23	    require_relative "ollama/models"
    24	
    25	    include RequestAdapter
    26	
    27	    HOST = "localhost"
    28	
    29	    ##
    30	    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    31	    def initialize(**)
    32	      super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @return [Symbol]
    37	    #  Returns the provider's name
    38	    def name
    39	      :ollama
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    ##
    43	    # Provides an embedding
    44	    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    45	    # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    46	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    47	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    48	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    49	    def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
    50	      params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
    51	      req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
    52	      req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
    53	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
    54	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
    55	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
    56	      res
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    ##
    60	    # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
    61	    # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
    62	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    63	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    64	    # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    65	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    66	    # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
    67	    #  When given an object a provider does not understand
    68	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    69	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    70	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    71	      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
    72	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
    73	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    74	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    75	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
    76	      res
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    ##
    80	    # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
    81	    # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
    82	    # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
    83	    def models
    84	      LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    ##
    88	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
    89	    def assistant_role
    90	      "assistant"
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    ##
    94	    # Returns the default model for chat completions
    95	    # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
    96	    # @return [String]
    97	    def default_model
    98	      "qwen3:latest"
    99	    end
   100	
   101	    private
   102	
   103	    def headers
   104	      lock do
   105	        (@headers || {}).merge(
   106	          "Content-Type" => "application/json",
   107	          "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
   108	        )
   109	      end
   110	    end
   111	
   112	    def stream_parser
   113	      LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
   114	    end
   115	
   116	    def error_handler
   117	      LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   121	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
   122	      tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   123	      params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   124	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   125	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   126	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   127	    end
   128	
   129	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
   130	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   131	      body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
   132	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
   133	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   134	      req
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   138	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   139	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
   140	      else
   141	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	  end
   145	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::Anthropic
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class ErrorHandler
     7	    ##
     8	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
     9	    #  Non-2XX response from the server
    10	    attr_reader :res
    11	
    12	    ##
    13	    # @return [Object, nil]
    14	    #  The span
    15	    attr_reader :span
    16	
    17	    ##
    18	    # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
    19	    #  The tracer
    20	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    21	    #  The span
    22	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    23	    #  The response from the server
    24	    # @return [LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler]
    25	    def initialize(tracer, span, res)
    26	      @tracer = tracer
    27	      @span = span
    28	      @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    29	    end
    30	
    31	    ##
    32	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    33	    #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
    34	    def raise_error!
    35	      ex = error
    36	      @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
    37	    ensure
    38	      raise(ex) if ex
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    private
    42	
    43	    ##
    44	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    45	    def error
    46	      if res.server_error?
    47	        LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
    48	      elsif res.unauthorized?
    49	        LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
    50	      elsif res.rate_limited?
    51	        LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
    52	      elsif res.not_found?
    53	        LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
    54	      else
    55	        LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
    56	      end
    57	    end
    58	  end
    59	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::Bedrock
     4	  ##
     5	  # Handles Bedrock API error responses.
     6	  #
     7	  # Bedrock errors come as JSON with:
     8	  #   { "message" => "...", "__type" => "..." }
     9	  # or as standard HTTP status codes.
    10	  #
    11	  # @api private
    12	  class ErrorHandler
    13	    ##
    14	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    15	    attr_reader :res
    16	
    17	    ##
    18	    # @return [Object, nil]
    19	    attr_reader :span
    20	
    21	    ##
    22	    # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
    23	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    24	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    25	    # @return [LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler]
    26	    def initialize(tracer, span, res)
    27	      @tracer = tracer
    28	      @span = span
    29	      @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    ##
    33	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    34	    def raise_error!
    35	      ex = error
    36	      @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
    37	    ensure
    38	      raise(ex) if ex
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    private
    42	
    43	    ##
    44	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    45	    def error
    46	      message = extract_message
    47	      if res.server_error?
    48	        LLM::ServerError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
    49	      elsif res.unauthorized?
    50	        LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
    51	      elsif res.forbidden?
    52	        LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
    53	      elsif res.rate_limited?
    54	        LLM::RateLimitError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
    55	      elsif res.not_found?
    56	        LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
    57	      else
    58	        LLM::Error.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
    59	      end
    60	    end
    61	
    62	    ##
    63	    # @return [String]
    64	    def extract_message
    65	      body = parse_body
    66	      body["message"] || body["Message"] || body["__type"] || "Unexpected error"
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    ##
    70	    # @return [Hash]
    71	    def parse_body
    72	      return {} if res.body.nil? || res.body.empty?
    73	      parsed = LLM.json.load(res.body.dup.force_encoding(Encoding::UTF_8).scrub)
    74	      Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
    75	    rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
    76	      {}
    77	    end
    78	  end
    79	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::Google
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class ErrorHandler
     7	    ##
     8	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
     9	    #  Non-2XX response from the server
    10	    attr_reader :res
    11	
    12	    ##
    13	    # @return [Object, nil]
    14	    #  The span
    15	    attr_reader :span
    16	
    17	    ##
    18	    # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
    19	    #  The tracer
    20	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    21	    #  The span
    22	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    23	    #  The response from the server
    24	    # @return [LLM::Google::ErrorHandler]
    25	    def initialize(tracer, span, res)
    26	      @tracer = tracer
    27	      @span = span
    28	      @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    29	    end
    30	
    31	    ##
    32	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    33	    #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
    34	    def raise_error!
    35	      ex = error
    36	      @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
    37	    ensure
    38	      raise(ex) if ex
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    private
    42	
    43	    ##
    44	    # @return [String, LLM::Object]
    45	    def body
    46	      @body ||= parse_body!
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    ##
    50	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    51	    def error
    52	      if res.server_error?
    53	        LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
    54	      elsif res.bad_request?
    55	        reason = body.dig("error", "details", 0, "reason")
    56	        if reason == "API_KEY_INVALID"
    57	          LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
    58	        else
    59	          LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
    60	        end
    61	      elsif res.rate_limited?
    62	        LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
    63	      elsif res.not_found?
    64	        LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
    65	      else
    66	        LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
    67	      end
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    ##
    71	    # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
    72	    # @return [String, LLM::Object]
    73	    def parse_body!
    74	      if String === res.body
    75	        LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
    76	      elsif Hash === res.body
    77	        LLM::Object.from(res.body)
    78	      else
    79	        res.body
    80	      end
    81	    rescue
    82	      res.body
    83	    end
    84	  end
    85	end

    49	        LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
    50	      elsif res.rate_limited?
    51	        LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
    52	      elsif res.not_found?
    53	        LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
    54	      else
    55	        LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
    56	      end
    57	    end
    58	  end
    59	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::OpenAI
     4	  ##
     5	  # @private
     6	  class ErrorHandler
     7	    ##
     8	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
     9	    #  Non-2XX response from the server
    10	    attr_reader :res
    11	
    12	    ##
    13	    # @return [Object, nil]
    14	    #  The span
    15	    attr_reader :span
    16	
    17	    ##
    18	    # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
    19	    #  The tracer
    20	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    21	    #  The span
    22	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    23	    #  The response from the server
    24	    # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
    25	    def initialize(tracer, span, res)
    26	      @tracer = tracer
    27	      @span = span
    28	      @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    29	    end
    30	
    31	    ##
    32	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    33	    #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
    34	    def raise_error!
    35	      ex = error
    36	      @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
    37	    ensure
    38	      raise(ex) if ex
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    private
    42	
    43	    ##
    44	    # @return [String, LLM::Object]
    45	    def body
    46	      @body ||= parse_body!
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    ##
    50	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    51	    def error
    52	      if res.server_error?
    53	        LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
    54	      elsif res.unauthorized?
    55	        LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
    56	      elsif res.rate_limited?
    57	        LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
    58	      elsif res.not_found?
    59	        LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
    60	      else
    61	        error = body["error"] || {}
    62	        case error["type"]
    63	        when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
    64	        when "server_error"
    65	          LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
    66	        else
    67	          LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
    68	        end
    69	      end
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # @param [Exception] error
    74	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    75	    def handle_invalid_request(error)
    76	      case error["code"]
    77	      when "context_length_exceeded"
    78	        LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
    79	      else
    80	        LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
    81	      end
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    ##
    85	    # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
    86	    # @return [String, LLM::Object]
    87	    def parse_body!
    88	      if String === res.body
    89	        LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
    90	      elsif Hash === res.body
    91	        LLM::Object.from(res.body)
    92	      else
    93	        res.body
    94	      end
    95	    rescue
    96	      res.body
    97	    end
    98	  end
    99	end
